Now entering it's 8th season, Facing Waves celebrates the cultures, landscapes and adventures that surround the world’s top paddling destinations. The popular TV series resonates with those who share a passion for the outdoors and seek out authentic experiences.

programme.title: Nurpu HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew for a dive into the wild world of whitewater kayaking.

programme.title: Ice Edge
programme.desc: Ice Edge is a short documentary about a group of surfers trying to make surfboards out of ice. It follows the process from freezing the boards, shaping them and taking them out into the sea. There is a lot of trying and failing. Will it work at all? The boards are slippery, and they melt very fast. Everything needs to work simultaneously: The surfers must be ready. The boards cannot crack. The perfect wave must come. Ice Edge is a film where nature and people play together.

programme.title: Seward
programme.desc: Tag along as TGR takes an amazing crew of new-school backcountry jibbers including Sage Cattabriga-Alosa and Kye Petersen to the pristine, ocean-view mountains of Seward, Alaska to see how they will use the landscape.

programme.title: Following Your Passions HD
programme.desc: Two legends of action sports Jeremy Jones and Selema Masekela sit down for an honest conversation about their careers and problems facing not only the action sports community but the world.

programme.title: Kakapo Crest
programme.desc: Tasmanian climbers Liz Oh and Rosie Hohnen are persuaded by filmmaker Olivia Page and a local kiwi stoat trapper, Ana Richards, to hike off-track deep into remote Fiordland, New Zealand, in search of a sabre-like peak. They surrender themselves to Ana's confidence in navigating them through terrain few have crossed, and in parts never traversed at all. They battle through vertical plants, gale force storms and dangerously loose rock - but not without humor.

programme.title: Connection HD
programme.desc: A highline project organized by passionate woman all around the globe. Eight girls decide to rig a half a kilometer long highline between two iconic rock towers in the Moab desert in Utah.

programme.title: Kayaking in the Laurentian Mountains
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he heads to the adventure-filled region of Québec that gets its' name from a chain of mountains the runs through it—the Laurentians. Join Ken as he explores the islands of Lac Poisson Blanc by sea kayak before putting his whitewater kayaking skills to the test on one of Eastern Canada's most popular rivers, the Rouge River.

programme.title: The Yukon Blues
programme.desc: Igor D’India’s epic journey down the Yukon River, across Canada & Alaska. 16000 km hitch-hiking and nearly 2000 km solo paddling with an “old-fashioned” approach to adventure. Without any assistance, radio, GPS or phone, just some solar panels to re-charge his audio/video equipment.

programme.title: All Or Nothing 2 HD
programme.desc: All Or Nothing 2 is a self supported project for the love of 2 wheel sports, filmed in the first six months of 2014 in countries such as Andorra, England, Portugal and Spain.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking Killarney Provincial Park
programme.desc: A World Champion kayaker, the author of twelve best-selling instructional books and videos on paddling, and a YouTube influencer with approximately 14 million views to his videos, Ken Whiting is the host of Facing Waves. Now entering it's 8th season, Facing Waves celebrates the cultures, landscapes and adventures that surround the world’s top paddling destinations. The popular TV series resonates with those who share a passion for the outdoors and seek out authentic experiences.

programme.title: Paddling Authentic Québec
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks and canoes the beautiful waters and incredible diversity of Québec Authentique. Located on the north shore of the St. Lawrence River, between Québec's two urban hubs of Montréal and Quebec City, Authentic Québec is a blending of the province's beautiful Lanaudière and Mauricie regions which offer incredible paddling opportunities in the many parks the regions are known for.

programme.title: Kayak Fishing Lake Erie
programme.desc: Fishing and kayaking is an unbeatable combination, as it allows you to fish in places inaccessible by boat or foot. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he meets up with Baer Fishing Adventures to chase pike and bass from their kayaks in the marshes surrounding Long Point Bay in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County.

programme.title: Baja HD
programme.desc: Follow Brian Conley, Ola Eleogram and the rest of the Teton Gravity Research crew as they find themselves in Baja California, Mexico during the making of TGR‚Äôs 2005 surf film Shack Therapy.

programme.title: Norway HD
programme.desc: Follow the TGR crew as they head north of the Arctic Circle. Doubts of finding good waves remain high after the first day of blown out waves, but forecasts of a swell keep the two motivated.

programme.title: Girdwood HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew as they head north to Girdwood, Alaska, home to some of the steepest and most exposed descents in North America, if not the world.

programme.title: Snowwater, BC
programme.desc: Join Dash Longe, Dylan Hood, and Peter Olenick as they head from Jackson, Wyoming to Snowwater, British Columbia and arrive to find a skier’s wish list of toys at their disposal: snowcat, snowmobiles and a helicopter.

programme.title: Siyinqaba
programme.desc: Nalle Hukkataival and Jimmy Webb recently traveled to Swaziland in search of new boulders and found an unforgettable country with amazing people and fascinating culture. But finding boulder problems isn’t as easy as it sounds, you might end up walking through fields and forest for hours and find nothing. Or you might find a gem, something that could possibly be THE next hard line, maybe even the African Star…

programme.title: Kwanza
programme.desc: Kwanza – The Drowning Diamond of Angola. When Olympic kayaker Mike Dawson heard rumors a river the size of the Zambezi hidden in West Africa he set in motion an ambitious plan to travel into the heart of Angola on the adventure of a life time to attempt the rivers wild rapids. ‘Kwanza’ is the story of that journey. An odyssey from the highlands of Zambia, through the deserts of Namibia and into the rawness of Angola.

programme.title: Kayaking the 'Canadian Amazon'
programme.desc: Big Creek in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County is commonly referred to as ‘the Canadian Amazon’ as it winds its way through thick forest, while presenting paddlers with a natural obstacle course of fallen trees. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores this ecological wonder from the seat of his kayak.

programme.title: Cooke City HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew as they head to Cooke City, Montana. Using locals as their guides, the crew finds cliffs to drop and straight-line some chutes while building tow-in booters.

programme.title: You Never Know
programme.desc: This documentary movie tells the story of one of the most successful female paragliding pilots in history. Klaudia shows throughout her career that if you really want something there is no way to stop you to make it happen. Regardless many difficulties she manages to win the World Champion Title in 2013. But she didn’t stop there. Winning medals isn’t the goal but progress, pursuit of personal excellence, sharing joy with friends and motivating others to follow their dreams is.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: Weyakin HD
programme.desc: Join a talented and ragtag band of kayakers as they seek out the wildest whitewater in the world.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: Creekbound and Down
programme.desc: Join up-and-coming professional whitewater kayakers Knox Hammack and Trent McCrerey as they travel around the American West in search of epic whitewater and glory, hitting all the rapids they can find along the way.

programme.title: Turkey HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew as they travel to Turkey, home to a surprisingly robust ski culture and some unique spots to film snow sports.

programme.title: Corsica
programme.desc: Tag along as the crew hunts lines in the high alpine of the Corsican Alps. Corsica is an island in the Mediterranean known more for its beaches than for its skiing, but that won't stop TGR from finding what they're looking for.

programme.title: A World of Injustice HD
programme.desc: The link between racial inequality and the outdoors. Even though Jeremy and Selema share a passion for snowboarding, their experience with the sport has not been synonymous.

programme.title: Pathfinder

Pathfinder is a tale about the human spirit.The film follows six world-class slackliners (Highliners) on a mission deep into the Norwegian mountains to attempt something that’s never been seen before: Walking a thin line, elevated in the vastness between two colossal cliffs, illuminated only by the mystical northern lights.

programme.title: Billy the Bear
programme.desc: Anyone who's seen TGR's 2015 bike film unReal remembers the scene in which Tom Van Steenbergen is chased up a singletrack by a massive brown bear. Take a look behind the scenes at how the segment was brought to life.

programme.title: Light the Wick
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew for a look behind the scenes of Light the Wick, TGR's 2010 film. Athletes and crew alike push the limits of what's possible in the world of ski films.

programme.title: La Famiglia: Marta Bassino
programme.desc: Every victory, shiny gold medal, and coveted crystal globe is built on a skier’s passion, dedication, and hard work chasing a childhood dream. For Italian athlete Marta Bassino, skiing is second nature and winning the obvious thing to do. But while she makes it all look easy, there is a whole well-oiled machine helping her carve her path to glory. Supported by her family and the Salomon family, Marta intends to write her name among the greatest.

programme.title: Such Is Life
programme.desc: In 2008 the ante was upped for progressive skiing and cinematography. This is a reflection on the dedication, perseverance, loyalty, and excitement the Rage Crew works for all year. Such is our lives. Such is life.

programme.title: South Africa HD
programme.desc: Follow the crew on their journey along the South African coastline with veteran surfers Andy Irons and Sunny Garcia, along with groms Ian Walsh, Jamie O‚ÄôBrien, Jordy Smith, TJ Barron and others.

programme.title: Paradise Waits
programme.desc: Sony Mind's Eye is a series of self-edits by Teton Gravity Research athletes. Episode 3 features footage captured while filming for Paradise Waits, TGR's 2015 snowsports film.

programme.title: White Wedding
programme.desc: In Jackson Hole, Wyoming, the relentless winter of 2017 was one for the record books. Relive this epic 600 inch season in Storm Show’s newest ski/ride/adrenaline film White Wedding. Highlights include: neck deep powder, massive airs & probable first descents, the show-stopping snowpocalypse event, and a 40 mile expedition into the heart of the Wind River Range.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking the Caves of the Magdalen Islands
programme.desc: A breathtaking chain of islands located far from anywhere else in the Gulf of the St. Lawrence, les Îles de la Madeleine are unlike anything you've ever seen. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks and swims along the stunning coastline, explores amazing sea caves and discovers the unique pleasures of island living.

programme.title: Wadi Rum
programme.desc: Set in the expansive landscape of Jordan’s distinctive national park, “Wadi Rum” documents the unlikely story of two Israeli climbers, an affable Bedouin Muslim, and a professional American climber as they work together to complete a climbing route up Wadi Rums’ largest rock formation. Three years ago, Israeli climbers Eliav (Eli) Nissan and Elad Omer began working on the ‘Sultan El-Mujahidin,’ in an effort to re-introduce this area back into the modern climbing community.

programme.title: Global Convoy
programme.desc: A bunch of penniless goons are determined to see the world and meet all the wondrous peoples it contains. They buy cars for £150 and totally clueless, set off across Europe, Central Asia, Siberia and then over to the USA and south to Argentina. Years from now, when academics intone about the DIY backlash to a modern society drowning in tech, then this film will be cited.

programme.title: The Mentor
programme.desc: Professional climber Marcus Garcia travels from his home in southwest Colorado’s San Juan Mountains to the limestone cliffs and spires of El Potrero Chico in northern Mexico to revisit his mentor’s final climb. In an emotional and powerful act of closure, he reflects on the accident that took him a decade to talk about—and climbs the route that took his mentor’s life.

programme.title: Micah Creek HD
programme.desc: Follow the Teton Gravity crew as they search for lines in Micah Creek, BC. After being trapped in the lodge for six days, they get a chance to take out the heli and hit some deep powder lines.

programme.title: Switzerland HD
programme.desc: Traveling through Switzerland by train, a crew including Mark Carter and Shroder Baker experience Roitschaggata, huck it off of many structures, and have a close call with an avalanche.

programme.title: Behind the OneShot
programme.desc: Join TGR, Anthill Films, and Brandon Semenuk for the production of his OneShot, one of the most-seen MTB segments of all time, originally featured in unReal, TGR's 2015 bike film.

programme.title: Go Fast Go North
programme.desc: The Race2Alaska is one of adventure racing's greatest competitions - challenging mariners to navigate 750 miles of BC's most infamous waterways by human or wind power alone. The rules are simple: no motors (no, not even as a safety precaution), no outside help or support, first boat over the line wins.. Follow Team Hot Mess, a group of 4 young sailors from BC, as they race the elements, and 46 other teams, to Ketchikan, Alaska.

programme.title: Morocco HD
programme.desc: Tag along with the TGR crew as they travel to Morocco, interacting with the very different culture as they make their way up into the Atlas Mountains.

programme.title: Ski Test
programme.desc: Join TGR's all-star crew of skiers at Squaw Valley, CA for a week of testing out the newest and most exciting skis on the market.

programme.title: Kayaking in the Laurentian Mountains
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he heads to the adventure-filled region of Québec that gets its' name from a chain of mountains the runs through it—the Laurentians. Join Ken as he explores the islands of Lac Poisson Blanc by sea kayak before putting his whitewater kayaking skills to the test on one of Eastern Canada's most popular rivers, the Rouge River.

programme.title: Backpacking Urban California
programme.desc: Showcasing the people, places and adventures that surround the world’s top backpacking trails, Epic Trails is an exciting and inspiring adventure travel series hosted by Eric Hanson. An avid backpacker, knowledgeable wilderness explorer and passionate adventurer, Eric has a strong presence in the online backpacking community and shares his adventures in an approachable and inspirational way.

programme.title: Backpacking Durango Colorado
programme.desc: Showcasing the people, places and adventures that surround the world’s top backpacking trails, Epic Trails is an exciting and inspiring adventure travel series hosted by Eric Hanson. An avid backpacker, knowledgeable wilderness explorer and passionate adventurer, Eric has a strong presence in the online backpacking community and shares his adventures in an approachable and inspirational way.

programme.title: A New Route
programme.desc: After being stopped on a risky ascent twice because of ice, Jeremy Jones attempts to traverse the face to a new route during the filming of Higher, Teton Gravity Research's 2014 snowboard film.

programme.title: The Storm
programme.desc: Join Jeremy Jones and the Teton Gravity Research crew during a dangerous and tumultuous trip to Alaska during the filming of Higher, TGR's 2014 snowboard film.

programme.title: Balkans Express
programme.desc: Take a trip though the eyes of the guys who set their minds on the skies. Get an inside view of their world of dreaming to fly. See what it takes behind the scenes to get to these incredible places. Witness the joy that is shared in these moments of pure bliss as they leave the Earth to experience flight. Following dreams takes some sweat and hard work but it all comes back to make an adventure of love.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: Two On K2
programme.desc: Follow Gerlinde Kaltenbrunner and her husband Ralf Dujmovits as they try to summit the K2, Gerlinde’s last remaining peak for completion of the “Crown Of The Himalayas” and see what love means above 8000 meters.

programme.title: Fighting For Climate
programme.desc: An accidental activist. Jeremy Jones never aspired to start a non-profit like Protect Our Winters. But when he noticed dramatic changes in the mountains due to a warming climate, he founded POW to give the snowsports industry a voice in the fight against climate change. In this episode, Jeremy and Selema reflect on POW's origins and what activism looks like in the current political climate.

programme.title: Ski Legends
programme.desc: Over two ski seasons (2018-19), Director Lois Neu travelled to several ski resorts in the Northwestern United States and Southwestern British Columbia, Canada in search of Alpine skiers over the age of 80! Ski Legends showcases three Alpine Skiers in their 80’s and 90’s and celebrates Owen Owens, Grace Oaks and Charlie Bunce, as they share their philosophies for remaining physically active as senior citizens, as well as, their skills, motivations and shared passion for the sport of Alpine Skiing

programme.title: Brooks Lake HD
programme.desc: Tag along with Mark Carter, Travis Rice, and Bryan Iguchi and journey to a remote lodge in Wyoming and explore lines in the Absoroka Range, one of the most untapped mountain ranges in the country.

programme.title: Paddling Bon Echo Provincial Park And The Thousand Islands
programme.desc: A World Champion kayaker, the author of twelve best-selling instructional books and videos on paddling, and a YouTube influencer with approximately 14 million views to his videos, Ken Whiting is the host of Facing Waves. Now entering it's 8th season, Facing Waves celebrates the cultures, landscapes and adventures that surround the world’s top paddling destinations. The popular TV series resonates with those who share a passion for the outdoors and seek out authentic experiences.

programme.title: Chamonix HD
programme.desc: Follow Jeremy Jones as he competes in (and wins) his first big mountain competition ever, solidifying himself as the best in his field as TGR catalogs the event and the epic after-party.

programme.title: Way Of Life (Behind the Line)
programme.desc: Join the athletes featured in Teton Gravity Research's 2013 film "Way Of Life" as they push the limits of what is possible on skis and snowboards on some of the world's gnarliest terrain.

programme.title: TGR Tested Bentonville
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew of bikers as they head down to Bentonville, Arkansas to test out a selection of the newest and most exciting mountain bikes on the market.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking the 1000 Islands
programme.desc: The crystal-clear waters of Lake Ontario are home to a spectacular group of islands known as the 1000 Islands – and there’s 1000 good reasons to visit. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores the must-see local paddling spots by sea kayak.

programme.title: Moana Waiwai
programme.desc: This film follows the FreeSwim journey of wahine (woman) Ally Davey as she takes on 70km of open ocean around the stunning Coromandel coastline over three days to bring awareness to the protection of New Zealand’s oceans. It does more than encapsulate stunning vistas and highlight the extraordinary New Zealand environment, but inspires people to be aware of the natural beauty that surrounds them and to protect it before it is too late. It’s an inspirational film, not an activist film.

programme.title: Whistler
programme.desc: Tag along as the TGR crew heads to Whistler, British Columbia to create an insane terrain park. While waiting for bluebird weather the athletes release some of their angst by doing some bungee jumping off a bridge.

programme.title: Disko
programme.desc: Eccentric French skipper Olivier Huin builds his own wooden boat and takes it into some of the most treacherous waters in the world. Skipper Olivier invites 8 crewmates to join him as he attempts to sail North from Virginia (USA) deep into the Arctic circle in search of the legendary Disko Bay.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: Almost Ablaze
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew for a look behind the scenes of Almost Ablaze, TGR's 2014 film. Athletes and crew alike push the limits of what's possible in the world of ski films.

programme.title: Kayak Fishing Lake Erie
programme.desc: Fishing and kayaking is an unbeatable combination, as it allows you to fish in places inaccessible by boat or foot. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he meets up with Baer Fishing Adventures to chase pike and bass from their kayaks in the marshes surrounding Long Point Bay in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County.

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Deep Water is a captivating exploration of fear, mentorship, and deep water soloing in panoramic Ha Long Bay, Vietnam with psicobloc pioneer Tim Emmett and competition climber Kyra Condie. Watch as Kyra learns from the master, tackles the fear of climbing high above the water, and then succeeds on one of the hardest deep water solos in Vietnam, The Lightning Crack, graded 5.13. Ha Long Bay, Vietnam is a scenic climbing venue with over 3000 limestone formations dotting the ocean bay.

programme.title: The World of Waves
programme.desc: “The World of Waves” is an astonishing look at the ocean and the waves it creates to all the corners of the world. This film is completely shot on 35mm film as well as on the Red and Phantom camera’s. The visuals are nothing short of mind blowing, backed up with a soundtrack that puts you right in the action. This film embraces images that will last in your mind for eternity. Never before have angles and images in the ocean been captured in pure raw form.

programme.title: Ballan
programme.desc: Biopic on Alessandro Ballan, the Italian cyclist who became World Champion in 2008, before the absurd disqualification for doping, subsequently being fully absolved after a long battle for the truth.

programme.title: Kayaking the 'Canadian Amazon'
programme.desc: Big Creek in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County is commonly referred to as ‘the Canadian Amazon’ as it winds its way through thick forest, while presenting paddlers with a natural obstacle course of fallen trees. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores this ecological wonder from the seat of his kayak.

programme.title: Way Of Life
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew for a look behind the scenes of Way Of Life, TGR's 2013 film. Athletes and crew alike push the limits of what's possible in the world of ski films.

programme.title: Hawaii HD
programme.desc: Join Maui surfer Ian Walsh and his tow-in partner Shane Dorian, and watch as the duo get the first big days of the 04/05 winter for tow-in surf at Jaws.

programme.title: Deeper Unplugged
programme.desc: Tag along as Jeremy Jones and his team travels the world in search of gnar while filming Deeper, Teton Gravity Research's 2010 snowboard film.

programme.title: Cape Horn
programme.desc: In 2009 three windsurfers and a film crew began sailing around the infamous Cape Horn. They wanted to capture the myth of Cape Horn and better understand the sailors and buccaneers of the old days, surrounding the southernmost tip of South America without engines or any kind of modern navigation system. They start from the Pacific coast in Chile, heading south towards the lakes of Patagonia and eventually coming to the end of the world in the Land of Fire.

programme.title: Far Out Ones
programme.desc: Join the all-star cast of Teton Gravity Research's award-winning 2018 film "Far Out" as they travel the world in search of deep powder, steep lines, and gnarly terrain to shred on their skis and snowboards

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: Otterbody
programme.desc: Join Jeremy Jones and the TGR team as they attempt to ride the Otterbody, a 55-degree patch of snow perched on the Grand Teton, while filming for Higher, TGR's 2014 snowboard film.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking the Saguenay Fjord
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he heads to Saguenay-Lac-Saint-Jean, home of the Saguenay–St. Lawrence Marine Park and the Parc national du Fjord-du-Saguenay—and one of the most dramatic landscapes in North America. Explore all that the amazing region has to offer as Ken sea kayaks alongside towering cliffs, sails through the heart of the fjord and whitewater kayaks down the world-class rivers Québec is known for.

programme.title: Kayaking the 'Canadian Amazon'
programme.desc: Big Creek in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County is commonly referred to as ‘the Canadian Amazon’ as it winds its way through thick forest, while presenting paddlers with a natural obstacle course of fallen trees. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores this ecological wonder from the seat of his kayak.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking Killarney
programme.desc: With stunning, coastal paddling and epic lake tripping, Killarney Provincial Park has something for kayakers and canoeists alike. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks a unique stretch of coastline known as Baie Fine – a freshwater fjord in the heart of Georgian Bay.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: TGR Tested Crested Butte
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew in Crested Butte, Colorado as they test and review several of the newest and most exciting mountain bikes on the market, focusing on technology and performance.

programme.title: Pathfinder
programme.desc: Life Beyond Fear. Pathfinder is a tale about the human spirit.The film follows six world-class slackliners (Highliners) on a mission deep into the Norwegian mountains to attempt something that’s never been seen before: Walking a thin line, elevated in the vastness between two colossal cliffs, illuminated only by the mystical northern lights.

programme.title: Norseman: A Day Of Extremes
programme.desc: Follow Caroline’s preparation and Norseman experience during 2022. This film follows her to Nepal where amongst the breathtaking scenery of the Himalayas she suffers with a medical emergency and is forced to return home. A few weeks later she competes in her home extreme triathlon. Celtman is held in the Highlands of Scotland where the weather and terrain mimic a little of what Norseman will hold. But can Caroline ever feel truly ready to take on the world’s toughest triathlon?

programme.title: Whitewater Canoeing the Madawaska River - Part 1
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he hops in a whitewater solo canoe for the first time ever and attempts to put his whitewater paddling skills to the test on the Madawaska River.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking Lake Mazinaw
programme.desc: Bon Echo is a small and extremely beautiful Provincial Park in Ontario's Highlands, which draws in visitors from all over thanks to the world class canoe tripping and kayak touring that it offers. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he takes in the dramatic cliff faces on Lake Mazinaw, which bear hundreds of Indigenous pictographs, from the seat of his sea kayak.

programme.title: Delta Of Spirit
programme.desc: Delta of Spirit explores the psyche and grit of adventurers who are willing to endure The DAMn, a grueling 240 mile gravel road bicycle race that traverses the state of Minnesota’s roads less traveled, all within 24 hours.

programme.title: Paddling The Petawawa River
programme.desc: A World Champion kayaker, the author of twelve best-selling instructional books and videos on paddling, and a YouTube influencer with approximately 14 million views to his videos, Ken Whiting is the host of Facing Waves. Now entering it's 8th season, Facing Waves celebrates the cultures, landscapes and adventures that surround the world’s top paddling destinations. The popular TV series resonates with those who share a passion for the outdoors and seek out authentic experiences.

programme.title: Kayaking The Rideau Canal
programme.desc: A World Champion kayaker, the author of twelve best-selling instructional books and videos on paddling, and a YouTube influencer with approximately 14 million views to his videos, Ken Whiting is the host of Facing Waves. Now entering it's 8th season, Facing Waves celebrates the cultures, landscapes and adventures that surround the world’s top paddling destinations. The popular TV series resonates with those who share a passion for the outdoors and seek out authentic experiences.

programme.title: Aspen HD
programme.desc: Tag along with Teton Gravity Research on a trip to Colorado, and watch as a crew throws down in Aspen, reaching the point where a jumping over a helicopter on a 95-foot jump is just another day.

programme.title: Mind The Gap
programme.desc: Join TGR and Anthill Films for a behind-the-scenes peek at the production and release of unReal, their 2015 mountain bike film.

programme.title: UTA: The Climb For Kosovo
programme.desc: In 2017, 34 year-old Uta Ibrahimi became the first person from Kosovo to attempt Everest. Born and raised in eastern Kosovo, her childhood was interrupted by a fierce ethnic war that ripped the heart out of the Balkan nation. Battling against Kosovo’s entrenched gender stereotypes and misogyny within the climbing community, Uta climbed the highest peaks in Kosovo, and then Europe.

programme.title: Homegrown Tour Guide
programme.desc: Get to know some of the coolest and most unique characters populating outdoors hubs around the country, and check out local favorite spots along the way.

programme.title: The KFC
programme.desc: The KFC - A Paragliding Adventure in Tanzania. Five Kiwis take a paragliding adventure in Tanzania, with the ultimate aim to fly from the summit of Mt Kilimanjaro.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking in Gaspésie, Québec
programme.desc: A massive and rugged peninsula surrounded by the Gulf of the St. Lawrence, Gaspésie features a dramatic coastline where beautiful wilderness rivers meet the ocean. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks around the iconic Percé Rock and stand up paddleboards the Bonaventure River.

programme.title: Alaska HD
programme.desc: Join Jeremy Jones, Sage Cattabriga-Alosa, Ian McIntosh, and Victoria Jealouse as they head to Alaska. Adverse snow conditions abound, but the experienced team manages to survive.

programme.title: Gunnie Season
programme.desc: The highly anticipated drop of 4bi9 Media’s “Gunnie Season” trailer is finally upon us. This is only 4bi9′s fourth feature length film, but you could consider them veterans in the game now. With a list of some of the top skiers in the world featured in their new film, it will be a must see. I recently sat down with the masterminds behind it all, AJ Dakoulas and Andrew Napier, and their assistant videographer/editor Kevin Steen for an interview about their season, and the making of Gunnie Season.

programme.title: The Mentor
programme.desc: Professional climber Marcus Garcia travels from his home in southwest Colorado’s San Juan Mountains to the limestone cliffs and spires of El Potrero Chico in northern Mexico to revisit his mentor’s final climb. In an emotional and powerful act of closure, he reflects on the accident that took him a decade to talk about—and climbs the route that took his mentor’s life.

programme.title: The Realm HD
programme.desc: The Realm brings you the ultimate documentation of freeriding‚Äôs relentless revolution. Explore Jackson Hole, Alaska, Utah, Japan, British Columbia, Europe, New Zealand, and Hawaii's Jaws.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking the Rideau Canal - Part 2
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he continues his houseboat and kayak trip along the Rideau River. Ken is joined by his wife and daughter, as they paddle through the locks of the canal and enjoy some quality family time on the water.

programme.title: Out Of Frame
programme.desc: Out Of Frame shows the daily life of Mathis Dumas, athlete, high mountain guide and outdoor photographer. A life suspended in the void, a camera in hand, Mathis takes us behind the scenes of a vertiginous highline shoot on a line never done before. A journey to the heart of the Mont Blanc massif where Mathis reveals his photographic vision and his mountain heritage outside the traditional framework.

programme.title: One For The Road
programme.desc: Join TGR's all-star athlete team as they travel the globe in search of deep snow, steep lines, and gnarly cliffs and couloirs on which to push the limits of skiing and snowboarding.

programme.title: Legacy - Surfing Now & Then
programme.desc: As the sport grows, more and more people surf. Do the surfing legends still relate to what pro surfing has become? Will surfing be as mainstream as other sports? Can you teach the Aloha Spirit to mainlanders and surfers from new demographics? Meet the surfing pioneers while they compete for a World Title in the Azores islands and get to know their views on what is still to come.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: Best of Untracked
programme.desc: Tag along with a number of Teton Gravity Research crews as they travel the world in search of the new and unknown throughout three seasons of Untracked, hitting all of the show's highlights.

programme.title: The Big One HD
programme.desc: The Big One celebrates a decade spent at and around Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, arguably the best ski resort in the world. Check out the highlights from years hammering "The Village."

programme.title: Kayaking in the Laurentian Mountains
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he heads to the adventure-filled region of Québec that gets its' name from a chain of mountains the runs through it—the Laurentians. Join Ken as he explores the islands of Lac Poisson Blanc by sea kayak before putting his whitewater kayaking skills to the test on one of Eastern Canada's most popular rivers, the Rouge River.

programme.title: The Bay Of Fundy: A Kayaking Adventure In Nova Scotia
programme.desc: Humpback whales inches from your kayak or multiple beat downs at the Walton ‘Wopper’ – these are some of the contrasting experiences captured on camera in Nova Scotia’s Bay of Fundy. Join top sea kayakers taking on the world’s largest tidal range; carving it up in the chocolately rapids of the Shubie river and seeing how long they can hold their breath in the meaty hole at Walton. Local paddler Chris Lockyer hosts Justine Curgenven and a crew of international sea kayaking talent.

programme.title: Rise Of Red
programme.desc: Join Tim Durtschi, Colter Hinchliffe, and Red, their Ford Ranger, as they travel across the West in search of late-season powder and adventure. It's a road trip unlike any other.

programme.title: Ski BASE
programme.desc: Tag along as Erik Roner walks us through his epic Cody Peak Ski BASE in Jackson Hole, Wyoming and then heads to Juneau, Alaska on a quest for even more ski BASE lines.

programme.title: Mountain In The Hallway
programme.desc: When Tate MacDowell and Brian McDonnell were diagnosed with cancer in 2016, they faced some tough realities. Nevertheless, they set their sights high: to beat cancer and climb the Grand Teton.

programme.title: Kayak Fishing Lake Erie
programme.desc: Fishing and kayaking is an unbeatable combination, as it allows you to fish in places inaccessible by boat or foot. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he meets up with Baer Fishing Adventures to chase pike and bass from their kayaks in the marshes surrounding Long Point Bay in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County.

programme.title: Turtle Ranch
programme.desc: Join TGR and Anthill Films as they turn Turtle Ranch into a bike park with the world's most advanced jumps and features and try to ride a ridge with a herd of thirty horses while filming for unReal, TGR's 2015 bike film.

programme.title: Immersion
programme.desc: Surfing is an amazing, enjoyable and incredible experience for everyone. But it is so much more than just a fun day at the beach for these kids. For three days in the summer in Maine, children with developmental disabilities, their families and their communities get together. It is here that bonds are made, and physical and mental change happens for all involved. The explosive joy has long lasting effects across all who attend.

programme.title: Re:Session
programme.desc: Learn more about some of the most iconic lines in Teton Gravity Research's 2009 film "Re:Session", featuring some of the world's most talented skiers and snowboarders pushing the limits of their sport.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking the 1000 Islands
programme.desc: The crystal-clear waters of Lake Ontario are home to a spectacular group of islands known as the 1000 Islands – and there’s 1000 good reasons to visit. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores the must-see local paddling spots by sea kayak.

programme.title: The Joe Louis Story HD
programme.desc: The life and career of heavyweight champion, Joe Louis. Not only did he battle opponents inside the ring, but he battled racism outside it as well.

programme.title: Dream Catcher Heli
programme.desc: Join the TGR crew as they board a boat which takes them deep into the British Columbia inlets where they use a helicopter to search for fresh terrain. After waiting for days, a break in the weather finally allows the boys to send it.

programme.title: Solastalgia
programme.desc: Solastalgia — a form of mental or existential distress caused by environmental change — is at the heart of this short documentary that reflects on climate change, nostalgia, and loss through the eyes of Serap, a Sherpa living and working as an Uber driver in New York City, far away from his hometown in Nepal. Although he moved to the U.S. eight years ago to provide a better education and safer life for his three children, his mind often drifts to the Himalayas that he misses tremendously.

programme.title: Kayaking the 'Canadian Amazon'
programme.desc: Big Creek in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County is commonly referred to as ‘the Canadian Amazon’ as it winds its way through thick forest, while presenting paddlers with a natural obstacle course of fallen trees. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores this ecological wonder from the seat of his kayak.

programme.title: Whitewater Canoeing the Madawaska River - Part 1
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he hops in a whitewater solo canoe for the first time ever and attempts to put his whitewater paddling skills to the test on the Madawaska River.

programme.title: Pathfinder
programme.desc: Life Beyond Fear. Pathfinder is a tale about the human spirit.The film follows six world-class slackliners (Highliners) on a mission deep into the Norwegian mountains to attempt something that’s never been seen before: Walking a thin line, elevated in the vastness between two colossal cliffs, illuminated only by the mystical northern lights.

programme.title: Snowboard Test
programme.desc: Join Teton Gravity Research's stacked team at Squaw Valley as they test out new snowboards from across the industry.

programme.title: Pathfinder
programme.desc: Life Beyond Fear. Pathfinder is a tale about the human spirit.The film follows six world-class slackliners (Highliners) on a mission deep into the Norwegian mountains to attempt something that’s never been seen before: Walking a thin line, elevated in the vastness between two colossal cliffs, illuminated only by the mystical northern lights.

programme.title: Welcome to Nain
programme.desc: In March 2019, Estski realized a backcountry skiing expedition in Nunatsiavut, an Inuit-owned autonomous territory of the Province of Newfoundland and Labrador (Canada). More specifically, in two areas around the village of Nain in Labrador: Fraser Canyon and Kiglapait mountains. These mountains and canyons are very little visited in winter and several locals even mentioned that they had never seen anyone ski these corridors.

programme.title: Paradice
programme.desc: In winter, the Alps turn into a paradise for top alpine sports. Huge waterfalls freeze to ice and leave unique formations. Paradice shows three of the most beautiful Swiss ice climbing tours from an unusual perspective. A film about risk, responsibility and the beauty of nature. A film featuring Jonas Schild, Nicolas Hojac and Stephan Siegrist.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking the 1000 Islands
programme.desc: The crystal-clear waters of Lake Ontario are home to a spectacular group of islands known as the 1000 Islands – and there’s 1000 good reasons to visit. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores the must-see local paddling spots by sea kayak.

programme.title: Canadian Stunts
programme.desc: Head up to Western Canada with TGR and Anthill Films as they do their best to film a segment despite adverse weather and injuries during the filming of unReal, TGR's 2015 bike film.

programme.title: Gone Tomorrow
programme.desc: Ice climbing in Kentucky?! This adventure documentary takes the audience for a bourbon-fueled ride deep into the bushy hollers of Appalachia with a crew of harmless misfits as they race to search out and climb new ice routes before they're gone, with a few surprises along the way.

programme.title: TGR Turns 21
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research team for a celebration of 21 years of pushing the limits of what's possible in winter sports. Featuring highlights from TGR's best segments, you shouldn't miss this!

programme.title: Andy Irons Kissed By God Raw Outtakes
programme.desc: Take a look behind the scenes at the characters involved in Andy Irons: Kissed By God, Teton Gravity Research's 2018 film about the late surfer's life. From neurological experts to Andy's closest friends and family, it's an intimate look at Andy's life.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking Lake Mazinaw
programme.desc: Bon Echo is a small and extremely beautiful Provincial Park in Ontario's Highlands, which draws in visitors from all over thanks to the world class canoe tripping and kayak touring that it offers. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he takes in the dramatic cliff faces on Lake Mazinaw, which bear hundreds of Indigenous pictographs, from the seat of his sea kayak.

programme.title: The Length Of Time: Inside The Tour Divide
programme.desc: The Tour Divide is a 2750 mile (4400 km) unsupported mountain bike race from Banff, Canada to Antelope Wells on the Mexican border. The Length of Time documents the race from a rider’s perspective telling the story of Josh Ibbett’s ride. See the emotional highs and lows of this classic bikepacking race from the inside.

programme.title: Russia HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew as they adventure through Russia. Despite friction with the government, and a string of questionable characters, the crew conquers Europe‚Äôs highest peak.

programme.title: Canoe Tripping In Killarney Provincial Park
programme.desc: A World Champion kayaker, the author of twelve best-selling instructional books and videos on paddling, and a YouTube influencer with approximately 14 million views to his videos, Ken Whiting is the host of Facing Waves. Now entering it's 8th season, Facing Waves celebrates the cultures, landscapes and adventures that surround the world’s top paddling destinations. The popular TV series resonates with those who share a passion for the outdoors and seek out authentic experiences.

programme.title: The Far North
programme.desc: Join TGR and Anthill Films on an adventure to the frigid north, where bikers will attempt to bring world-class tricks to a frozen and glaciated landscape while filming for unReal, TGR's 2015 bike film.

programme.title: Tulsequah, BC HD
programme.desc: Tag along as the crew sets up in the wilderness of Tulsequah, BC. Utilizing the expertise of some of the world‚Äôs foremost big mountain riders, the crew finds first ascents through dangerous terrain.

programme.title: Grand Teton
programme.desc: Jeremy Jones heads to Jackson Hole to attempt to ride the Otterbody in Teton Park. Jeremy and the Teton Gravity Research team will face the harrowing climb to the summit of the Grand Teton.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking the Rideau Canal - Part 1
programme.desc: A UNESCO world heritage site, the Rideau Canal runs through the heart of Canada’s capital city of Ottawa. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he takes a different approach to see the canal by exploring a larger section of the 126-mile waterway by houseboat and kayak.

programme.title: Ice Edge
programme.desc: Ice Edge is a short documentary about a group of surfers trying to make surfboards out of ice. It follows the process from freezing the boards, shaping them and taking them out into the sea. There is a lot of trying and failing. Will it work at all? The boards are slippery, and they melt very fast. Everything needs to work simultaneously: The surfers must be ready. The boards cannot crack. The perfect wave must come. Ice Edge is a film where nature and people play together.

programme.title: Bulgaria HD
programme.desc: Join the TGR crew as they explore a local mountain resort in Bulgaria called Bansko. The crew then hits the mountain tops, jumping huge cliffs and shooting narrow couloirs.

programme.title: The Chase (2020)
programme.desc: Starring Mitchell Blewitt and filmed over four years across locations including; Tahiti, Cook Islands, South Australia, Tasmania and N.S.W, The Chase explores the essence of what it means to be a bodyboarder who lives to chase for incredible oceanic moments and the adventures that surround them.

programme.title: The Chamber: Exploring The World's Greatest Cave
programme.desc: The film documents the discovery and exploration of Sarawak Chamber in Nasib Bagus cave in the Mulu National Park, Sarawak, Borneo. Since its discovery in 1980 Sarawak Chamber remains the largest cave chamber yet discovered and is the largest unsupported structure on earth either natural or man made. Following the original explorers as they return to the chamber 30 years later.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking in Gaspésie, Québec
programme.desc: A massive and rugged peninsula surrounded by the Gulf of the St. Lawrence, Gaspésie features a dramatic coastline where beautiful wilderness rivers meet the ocean. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks around the iconic Percé Rock and stand up paddleboards the Bonaventure River.

programme.title: Dream Job
programme.desc: A comedy documentary about a girl working in the ski industry who decides to become one of the stars. DREAM JOB begs the question – they go up, they go down, how hard can it be? The film follows comedian Katie Burrell through “days in the life” with three of the industry’s biggest names: Leah Evans, founder of Girls Do Ski, Christina Lustenberger, ex-Olympian and big mountain skier, and Diny Harrison, the first certified female mountain guide in North America.

programme.title: One For The Road
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew as they dissect and explain lines from One For The Road, TGR's 2011 snowsports film.

programme.title: Ice Edge
programme.desc: Ice Edge is a short documentary about a group of surfers trying to make surfboards out of ice. It follows the process from freezing the boards, shaping them and taking them out into the sea. There is a lot of trying and failing. Will it work at all? The boards are slippery, and they melt very fast. Everything needs to work simultaneously: The surfers must be ready. The boards cannot crack. The perfect wave must come. Ice Edge is a film where nature and people play together.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking in Gaspésie, Québec
programme.desc: A massive and rugged peninsula surrounded by the Gulf of the St. Lawrence, Gaspésie features a dramatic coastline where beautiful wilderness rivers meet the ocean. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks around the iconic Percé Rock and stand up paddleboards the Bonaventure River.

programme.title: The Dream Factory
programme.desc: Join TGR's all-star athlete team as they travel the globe in search of deep snow, steep lines, and gnarly cliffs and couloirs on which to push the limits of skiing and snowboarding.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: Brown Pow Day
programme.desc: Head to British Columbia with TGR and Anthill Films as crew and riders alike attempt to envision the biker's equivalent of a powder day while filming for unReal, TGR's 2015 bike film.

programme.title: Utah HD
programme.desc: Tag along as Teton Gravity Research takes a huge crew into the light and fluffy snow of Utah and is blessed with some of the best early season conditions that Utah has to offer.

programme.title: Inside the Film Accomplice
programme.desc: Join the world's most talented mountain bikers on a whirlwind tour around the globe and get a glimpse behind the scenes of Teton Gravity Research's 2020 MTB film ACCOMPLICE, with a focus on characters and technology.

programme.title: Ski BASE HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew for a close look at the thrilling and dangerous world of ski BASE-jumping.

programme.title: Italia HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity crew as they head to Italy. After Micah, Meg, and Jeremy enjoy some fresh powder in Cinque Torri, they meet up with skier and BASE jumper Erik Roner, who proceeds to blow minds.

programme.title: Dangers of the Summit
programme.desc: Tag along as Jeremy Jones attempts to climb and ride Shangri-La, a frankly ridiculous "line" on a remote Himalayan peak while filming for Higher, Teton Gravity Research's 2014 snowboard film.

programme.title: Setbacks
programme.desc: Join Jeremy Jones and the TGR team on an expedition to the Himalayas during the production of Higher, TGR's 2014 snowboard film. Adverse weather and altitude sickness plague the team.

programme.title: Caroline Islands HD
programme.desc: Witness the seasoned fearlessness of Pancho Sullivan, and the raw talent of Raoni Monteiro, Kieren Perrow and Darren O‚ÄôRafferty as they take on rarely-seen waves in the remote Caroline Islands.

programme.title: Switzerland HD
programme.desc: Switzerland has some of the biggest and gnarliest ski runs on the planet. Follow Micah Black, Marc Andre Belliveau, Skogen Sprang, and the TGR crew as they venture into the unknown.

programme.title: Togwotee HD
programme.desc: Follow the Teton Gravity Research crew as they go riding in Togwotee Pass and all throughout the Continental Divide, traveling hundreds of miles on snowmobiles in search of new zones and fresh powder.

programme.title: Paradise Waits
programme.desc: Join TGR's all-star athlete team as they travel the globe in search of deep snow, steep lines, and gnarly cliffs and couloirs on which to push the limits of skiing and snowboarding.

programme.title: Richard Permin: Chroniques D'Hiver
programme.desc: Freeride skier Richard Permin has traveled the world during winter to bring back the most beautiful images possible for over 10 years by living in the shadows and solely focusing on performance. Since 2016 he has been working with director Maxime Moulin and after two big projects (Good Morning and Fastwood) it was time to do things differently and simply enjoy the mountains.

programme.title: Kayaking the 'Canadian Amazon'
programme.desc: Big Creek in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County is commonly referred to as ‘the Canadian Amazon’ as it winds its way through thick forest, while presenting paddlers with a natural obstacle course of fallen trees. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores this ecological wonder from the seat of his kayak.

programme.title: House Of The Gods
programme.desc: The great northern prow of Mount Roraima soars from the depths of the Amazon rainforest like the prow of a gigantic ship. Shrouded in cloud, drenched by torrential rain and guarded by vertical jungle, deep mud and overhanging cliffs, local legend has it that only those of pure soul will ever see the summit of Mount Roraima.

programme.title: Pathfinder
programme.desc: Life Beyond Fear. Pathfinder is a tale about the human spirit.The film follows six world-class slackliners (Highliners) on a mission deep into the Norwegian mountains to attempt something that’s never been seen before: Walking a thin line, elevated in the vastness between two colossal cliffs, illuminated only by the mystical northern lights.

programme.title: On the Verge
programme.desc: In the mountains behind the sleepy coastal town of Powell River, BC, a small group of rock climbers has spent decades quietly pioneering routes on some of the largest granite walls in Canada. As the last stands of old-growth trees harboured in these valleys come under threat of logging, the climbing community faces the uncertain future of a place that has come to define their lives and legacies.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking Lake Mazinaw
programme.desc: Bon Echo is a small and extremely beautiful Provincial Park in Ontario's Highlands, which draws in visitors from all over thanks to the world class canoe tripping and kayak touring that it offers. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he takes in the dramatic cliff faces on Lake Mazinaw, which bear hundreds of Indigenous pictographs, from the seat of his sea kayak.

programme.title: The Mega-Gap
programme.desc: Join Teton Gravity Research and Anthill Films as they build a 70-foot gap for Tom Van Steebergen to go as big as he has ever gone on his mountain bike while filming for unReal, TGR's 2015 bike film.

programme.title: The Himalayas
programme.desc: Join Jeremy Jones and the Teton Gravity Research team as they set out on their last and most difficult expedition: Trekking high into the Himalayan Mountains of Nepal for Higher, TGR's 2014 snowboard film.

programme.title: P3: Pedal Powder Paddle
programme.desc: Three friends from Canmore Alberta set out to paddle board the headwaters of one of Canada’s longest rivers, the north Saskatchewan. To get there they will have to bike, hike, ski and snowboard their way across two glaciers and one 11,000’ mountain ….all in one epic day.

programme.title: The Mentor
programme.desc: Professional climber Marcus Garcia travels from his home in southwest Colorado’s San Juan Mountains to the limestone cliffs and spires of El Potrero Chico in northern Mexico to revisit his mentor’s final climb. In an emotional and powerful act of closure, he reflects on the accident that took him a decade to talk about—and climbs the route that took his mentor’s life.

programme.title: Resilient
programme.desc: Life flourishes when you can learn to push down barriers and challenges that can be thrown at you out of nowhere. Jerad Fischer, Lindsey Runkel, and Dan Soller are the living proof of how you can keep shredding and attacking life with a positive and tenacious attitude after a life-changing injury. From Colorado local dirt jumps, to Highland Mountain, to the Adaptive MTB World Champs, “Resilient” will prove that we are all capable of what we put our minds to! Finalist – VIMFF 2020.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: WEMTB
programme.desc: WEMTB is a 30 minutes Mountain Bike project filmed in Spain and 2 islands, Tenerife and Ibiza, with top Spanish riders such as Toni Ferreiro, Bernat Guardia, Edgar Carballo, Gabriel Torralba, Iago Garay and many more. Including sisters, brothers, father and son, a wolf dog and Pablo Alonso pedalling against Parkinson´s disease. Nature, family, self improvement and beautiful trails. Filmed and produced by Dirtyflows in 2018 and 2019.

programme.title: Kayaking the 'Canadian Amazon'
programme.desc: Big Creek in Southern Ontario's Norfolk County is commonly referred to as ‘the Canadian Amazon’ as it winds its way through thick forest, while presenting paddlers with a natural obstacle course of fallen trees. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores this ecological wonder from the seat of his kayak.

programme.title: Paddling Opemican National Park
programme.desc: With more than 20,000 lakes and rivers and endless pristine wilderness, the Abitibi-Témiscamingue region in Southwestern Québec is a paddler's paradise.

programme.title: Kayaking in the Laurentian Mountains
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he heads to the adventure-filled region of Québec that gets its' name from a chain of mountains the runs through it—the Laurentians. Join Ken as he explores the islands of Lac Poisson Blanc by sea kayak before putting his whitewater kayaking skills to the test on one of Eastern Canada's most popular rivers, the Rouge River.

programme.title: Ocean Valour: Salcombe - New York HD
programme.desc: On 3rd May 2015, Tom Rainey and Lawrence Walters set out from Manhattan, New York to row 4,100 miles unsupported across the North Atlantic Ocean.

programme.title: Chile
programme.desc: Tag along as Teton Gravity Research takes pipe-jock snowboarders Pat Moore and Danny Davis to southern Chile to test their skills off-piste. The team explores the Termas de Chillan resort, and uses snowmobiles to access the vast Chilean alpine.

programme.title: Haines HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research team in Haines, Alaska. Victoria Jealouse hits spines, Sage Cattabriga-Alosa gets the line of his life, and Erik Roner falls hundreds of feet.

programme.title: Paddling the Saint Laurence River
programme.desc: Known for its spectacular St. Lawrence coastline and rugged wilderness, Bas-Saint-Laurent, Québec is an outdoor lover's dream. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores the region by sea kayak in Bic National Park, before hopping in a canoe to see bald eagles up close and personal in Lake Témiscouata National Park.

programme.title: The Mentor
programme.desc: Professional climber Marcus Garcia travels from his home in southwest Colorado’s San Juan Mountains to the limestone cliffs and spires of El Potrero Chico in northern Mexico to revisit his mentor’s final climb. In an emotional and powerful act of closure, he reflects on the accident that took him a decade to talk about—and climbs the route that took his mentor’s life.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking Québec's North Shore
programme.desc: Côte-Nord, Québec is a seemingly boundless area that's known for its rugged beauty and diversity of its wildlife. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he goes sea kayaking with puffins and monoliths in Mingan Archipelago National Park Reserve, as well as stand up paddles and whitewater kayaks one of the many rivers that drops into the Gulf of the Saint Lawrence River.

programme.title: Ice Edge
programme.desc: Ice Edge is a short documentary about a group of surfers trying to make surfboards out of ice. It follows the process from freezing the boards, shaping them and taking them out into the sea. There is a lot of trying and failing. Will it work at all? The boards are slippery, and they melt very fast. Everything needs to work simultaneously: The surfers must be ready. The boards cannot crack. The perfect wave must come. Ice Edge is a film where nature and people play together.

programme.title: Jackson Hole HD
programme.desc: Join the Teton Gravity Research crew as they rip around Jackson Hole Mountain Resort and surrounding backcountry during the filming of TGR's Tangerine Dream.

programme.title: Backpacking Uganda Part 1
programme.desc: Showcasing the people, places and adventures that surround the world’s top backpacking trails, Epic Trails is an exciting and inspiring adventure travel series hosted by Eric Hanson. An avid backpacker, knowledgeable wilderness explorer and passionate adventurer, Eric has a strong presence in the online backpacking community and shares his adventures in an approachable and inspirational way.

programme.title: Numinous: A Ski Film
programme.desc: He is one of the most aggressive and talented freeskiers of our age. Born and raised in the BC backcountry, with a bloodline alive with adventure and a style carved from the landscape itself, Kye Petersen is about to blow the doors off of big mountain skiing. Numinous explores the relationships and connections with the natural world that are necessary to safely dance with mountain faces covered in snow.

programme.title: Kayaking Québec City
programme.desc: Join host, Ken Whiting, as he sea kayaks along the Jacques Cartier River before heading to Québec City to explore the cobblestone streets and old buildings the UNESCO World Heritage Site is known for.

programme.title: Backpacking Uganda Part 2
programme.desc: Showcasing the people, places and adventures that surround the world’s top backpacking trails, Epic Trails is an exciting and inspiring adventure travel series hosted by Eric Hanson. An avid backpacker, knowledgeable wilderness explorer and passionate adventurer, Eric has a strong presence in the online backpacking community and shares his adventures in an approachable and inspirational way.

programme.title: CLIMB
programme.desc: Santa Barbara triathlete Neil Myers was nearly killed in a horrific training accident on Gibraltar Road in 2018. One week after the accident, he signed up to compete in a triathlon that was a year away, with a goal of simply crossing the finish line. Four months after healing and leaving rehab, Neil began his cycling training. Only one year after the accident, he surpassed the goal he had set while he was in the hospital. He and his team won the 2019 Santa Barbara Triathlon.

programme.title: Sea Kayaking the 1000 Islands
programme.desc: The crystal-clear waters of Lake Ontario are home to a spectacular group of islands known as the 1000 Islands – and there’s 1000 good reasons to visit. Join host, Ken Whiting, as he explores the must-see local paddling spots by sea kayak.

programme.title: Solving for Z | A Calculus of Risk
programme.desc: How much can we gain from the mountains? How much can we lose? Between our own perceptions and the reality of the hazards, how much space exists? And in that gap, how do we decide what we are willing to risk? Solving for Z explores IFMGA guide and father Zahan Billimoria’s relationship to the intoxicating highs and crushing blows of a life in the high-consequence environment of big mountain skiing.

programme.title: Pathfinder
programme.desc: Life Beyond Fear. Pathfinder is a tale about the human spirit.The film follows six world-class slackliners (Highliners) on a mission deep into the Norwegian mountains to attempt something that’s never been seen before: Walking a thin line, elevated in the vastness between two colossal cliffs, illuminated only by the mystical northern lights.

programme.title: Paddling Norfolk County Ontario
programme.desc: A World Champion kayaker, the author of twelve best-selling instructional books and videos on paddling, and a YouTube influencer with approximately 14 million views to his videos, Ken Whiting is the host of Facing Waves. Now entering it's 8th season, Facing Waves celebrates the cultures, landscapes and adventures that surround the world’s top paddling destinations. The popular TV series resonates with those who share a passion for the outdoors and seek out authentic experiences.

programme.title: Night Bike
programme.desc: Brett Rheeder's segment was always going to be unReal's opening segment, so the pressure was on as he shot in the Canadian nightime. Despite a few struggles, he more than earned his spot in unReal, TGR's 2015 bike film.

programme.title: The Foot-Powered Transition HD
programme.desc: Leap of Faith. What happens when the ‚Äòdream job‚Äô no longer fills up your cup? Jeremy and Selema explore the defining moment in their careers when they had to risk it all and pursue their passions.

programme.title: Sail For Surf
programme.desc: Collin Martins is a chef with only one goal in mind: surfing. The virus started at the age of 3 when his parents put him on a surfboard in Cap Ferret. It often starts like this. But the course of Collin and his passion for surfing is really atypical and unique. In his quest for a wave, he decides to cross a step in 2017 when he wanted to be autonomous on the peninsula with a sailboat.

programme.title: The B Team- Who's Number One Part 1

programme.title: The B Team- Who's Number One Part 2
programme.desc: Check out The B-Team getting ready for Who’s Number One competitions. Follow the squad as they weigh in, check in with the press, and make the final preparations before battle. Thanks for watching, keep it locked on The Grappling Network

programme.title: ADCC - Denver Opens
programme.desc: The Opens make their way to the mile high city where the best martial arts from the US look to stake their claim for supremacy as the circuit heats up. In this heart-pounding instalment, we bring you all the action from the epic showdown held in the magnificent city of Denver. Former UFC Strawweight Champion of the World Rose Namajunas known for her exceptional martial arts abilities, Rose is now ready to put her skills to the ultimate test in the realm of grappling. Witness her transition from the cage to the mats, as she grapples her way through some of the most intense matches of her career. ADCC Opens superstar and prodigy 16-year-old phenom, Helena Craver, steps onto the mats to showcase her unparalleled talent. With her sights set on glory, she aims to conquer the teenage, adults, and absolute divisions a feat that has never been done before. In a clash of titans, long time elite grappler Ryan Aitkens, a formidable competitor in his own right, is set to go head-to-head against the multiple-time world champion, Mateus Lutes. Brace yourself for an explosive battle between these two grappling juggernauts, as they leave everything on the mats in pursuit of victory.

programme.title: American Grappling - Los Angeles
Breaking - Rules of Engagement - Peach's Bachelor Jam
programme.desc: "Tune in for an exciting 1v1 battles format at Rules of Engagement! A few of the best up and coming breakers face off to see who will reign supreme. Shot by BBoyNetwork"

programme.title: CPI - Sean O'Malley Recovers with Stem Cells
programme.desc: Anthony Kadel and Michael Plaster go to The Cellular Performance Institute in Tijuana, Mexico to cover the UFC's #1 ranked Bantamweight, "Suga" Sean O'Malley and his Coach Tim Welch. Spend a day with Sean and Tim as they embark on the road to recovery at the premier stem cell hospital. Presented by The Grappling Network.

programme.title: CPI Podcast - Sean O'Malley & Tim Welch
programme.desc: CPI Stem Cell Co-Founder Scotty Nelson hosts episode 4 of The CPI Podcast with UFC all-star "Suga" Sean O'Malley and Tim Welch, both flying out from Phoenix, AZ for treatment at @CPIStemCells Sean came down to get stem cell injections in a knee that had been bothering him for years and Tim opted for shoulder injections and a full-body stem cell IV. These treatments have been changing the way combat athletes heal & recover and we are happy to have been able to serve these awesome guys!

programme.title: American Grappling - Los Angeles
American Grappling - Seattle
programme.desc: American Grappling heads to Seattle, Washington. Coach Morgan heads to the world-famous Pike’s Market and get a taste of the Northwest. Our featured competitor is the black belt Nathan Orchard. Nathan is looking to make Team USA to tick the final box of his career…win a grappling world championship…but he’ll have to get past Eric Spence. Eric has wrestled his whole life and competed on a wrestling US World team. It’s the traditional clash of styles between wrestling and jiujitsu. Find out who’ll end up on top and secure their spot at the world team trials.

programme.title: Stuart Cooper Films - The Craig Jones Story
programme.desc:
A short documentary telling the story of Craig Jones' sudden rise to success after his upset victory over Leandro Lo at ADCC 2017. After his breakout performance at ADCC, Craig went on to beat many more big names such as Jake Shields In Polaris, and made it to the finals of EBI, where he had a close finals match against Gordon Ryan. Since then Craig has competed in some of the biggest tournaments in the world and his life has changed dramatically. In this film we see that it wasn't always an easy road for him.
Director Stuart Cooper

programme.title: Superfight - Andre Galvao vs Braulio Estima by Stuart Cooper
programme.desc: In this video Andre Galvao looks back on his match with Braulio Estima for the ADCC 2013 Super Fight Title. Then, Andre recaps on some of his most entertaining matches from ADCC 2011, which include; Don Ortega, Gunnar Nelson, Pablo Popovitch, Rousimar "Toquinho" Palhares and Sergio Moraes.

programme.title: Tap Out Challenge - Oceanside
programme.desc: On October 15th at the picturesque Oceanside, CA, Charles Rials embarked on an incredible BJJ journey, challenging strangers from all walks of life to tap me out and claim $100. But there was a twist that none of them saw coming! Win or Lose, they got the MONEY!!! What they all shared was the courage to step up, the passion to engage, and the heart of a warrior. This video isn't just about BJJ or the challenge — it's a celebration of the human spirit, perseverance, and the unexpected twists that life (and jiu jitsu) can throw our way. Join Charles in this heartwarming, adrenaline-pumping journey where every challenge was met with respect, and every challenger was met with a surprise. Whether you're a BJJ enthusiast or someone who loves to witness genuine human moments, this video has something special for you.

programme.desc: Brandon McCaghren goes over Rubber Guard. It is another staple of the 10th planet system. BMAC has some IMPORTANT details to keep in mind when playing this game.

programme.title: Ryan Hall - Philosophy of Jiu Jitsu
programme.desc: Black Belt under 2-time world champion, Felipe Costa, Ryan Hall is one of the most competitively successful American-born Jiu-Jitsu players to date and is widely regarded as one of the best lightweight submission grapplers in the world.

programme.title: Shugyo Invitational - Episode 1
programme.desc: The Shugyo Invitational is a round robin, submission only, no time limit tournament. This episode features; Nathan Orchard, Shawn Weisenburgh, Jordan Holy and Rafa Domingos.

programme.desc: The Shugyo Invitational is a round robin, submission only, no time limit tournament. This episode features; Keith Krikorian, Josh Bacallao, Max Rohskopf and Ethan Crelinsten.

programme.title: Stuart Cooper Films - The Craig Jones Story
programme.desc:
A short documentary telling the story of Craig Jones' sudden rise to success after his upset victory over Leandro Lo at ADCC 2017. After his breakout performance at ADCC, Craig went on to beat many more big names such as Jake Shields In Polaris, and made it to the finals of EBI, where he had a close finals match against Gordon Ryan. Since then Craig has competed in some of the biggest tournaments in the world and his life has changed dramatically. In this film we see that it wasn't always an easy road for him.
Director Stuart Cooper

programme.title: Superfight - Andre Galvao vs Braulio Estima by Stuart Cooper
programme.desc: In this video Andre Galvao looks back on his match with Braulio Estima for the ADCC 2013 Super Fight Title. Then, Andre recaps on some of his most entertaining matches from ADCC 2011, which include; Don Ortega, Gunnar Nelson, Pablo Popovitch, Rousimar "Toquinho" Palhares and Sergio Moraes.

programme.title: Tap Out Challenge - Oceanside
programme.desc: On October 15th at the picturesque Oceanside, CA, Charles Rials embarked on an incredible BJJ journey, challenging strangers from all walks of life to tap me out and claim $100. But there was a twist that none of them saw coming! Win or Lose, they got the MONEY!!! What they all shared was the courage to step up, the passion to engage, and the heart of a warrior. This video isn't just about BJJ or the challenge — it's a celebration of the human spirit, perseverance, and the unexpected twists that life (and jiu jitsu) can throw our way. Join Charles in this heartwarming, adrenaline-pumping journey where every challenge was met with respect, and every challenger was met with a surprise. Whether you're a BJJ enthusiast or someone who loves to witness genuine human moments, this video has something special for you.

programme.title: PaddleSUP Mission Amazon
programme.desc: Charlie Head will attempt the first ever stand-up paddle boarding of the main water source of the Amazon, the Rio Marañón in Peru. It’s never been attempted before and will be the most arduous and longest SUP journey ever made.Two months of using just his physical and mental strength, Charlie will overcome extreme terrain, trek around impassable river rock falls, survive freezing waters, high altitude, swarms of insects and suspicious local tribes.However, all the hardships will be worth it if he can help shine some light on one of the planet’s most endangered environments and educates us on conservation as over 20 new dams are planned to be constructed on the river's free-flowing stretches. This could have, according to scientists, drastic consequences on the Amazon basin forever.If Charlie does not embark on this adventure now, he may never be able to do so. Find out more with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Facing Waves - Episode 7
programme.desc: Poland is a place of immense historical significant that goes back more than a thousand years. Although it is well known for its role in the Second World War, Poland is also being appreciated for its world class cuisine, breathtaking countryside and ease of travel. This week on Facing Waves, we are set to discover the beautiful waterways of Poland.

programme.title: Bol D'Or: The Swiss Do It Better
programme.desc: The Bol D’Or Mirabaud is the most important inland lake regatta in the world. Taking place in Switzerland, a landlocked nation that sits on top of the sailing world with multiple successes whether in the Olympic, Offshore or in Grand Prix Racing formats, and champion of two America’s Cup victories. The race started in 1939 and it is a symbolic race that brings together the best of Switzerland’s sailing tradition.Every year they welcome mono-hulls and multihulls on Lake Geneva, starting in the city of Geneva. More than 500 boats fight each other on this 123 kilometre (66.5 nautical miles) course from Geneva to Le Bouveret and back. It is a national treasure that every summer gathers thousands of spectators to the shores of Lake Geneva and really shows how the Swiss indeed do it better.Watch the race and become one with the beauty of Switzerland’s background, available on Nautical Channel.
