programme.title: PRADA Cup - Semi-Final
programme.desc: As INEOS Team UK won the Round Robins, now it is the time for Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli and NYYC American Magic to compete on this semi-final of the 2021 Prada Cup. The first to win four races will become the final contender against the British team in this competition. Who will that be?

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 1
programme.desc: On this first episode of Inside Superyachts we meet with the Godfather of Superyacht builders – Feadship Yachts. Established in 1849, this Dutch shipyard is not only the number one in the industry but also the most famous of all superyacht builders, having created masterpieces for the likes of Steve Jobs.From the Netherlands we take a trip down to the ultra-discreet Principality of Monaco, host of the greatest yacht show in the world ‘The Monaco Yacht Show’. Then, we travel thousands of feet below the sea, exploring the vast ocean with Triton Submarines, utilized by some of films greatest names such as film director James Cameron and presenter David Attenborough.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 2
programme.desc: From the Godfather of builders to the Grandaddy of designers we visit the studio of Bannenberg & Rowell to unveil the legend that is the late great Jon Bannenberg. Following the winds of change we discuss the growing demand for explorer yachts. We meet London based H2 Design, and learn about the groundbreaking 107m explorer M/Y Ulysses.We continue our theme with EYOS Explorer and DAMEN yachts, the largest and most prolific ship builder on the planet.

programme.title: Jet Ski World Series - Round 3 - Thailand
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to Sailing to Tokyo 2020! We begin this first episode by travelling to Sorento in Melbourne, Australia, where the 2020 RS:X Windsurfing World Championships took place. A fleet of 116 sailors from 31 nations gathered in Sorento Sailing Couta Boat Club to challenge for the prestigious titles. Later on, we examine how the Coronavirus pandemic affected an athlete's routine. With the majority of the countries instituting some sort of stay-at-home order, public gatherings of any kind were banned, and numerous sport events were postponed and cancelled.

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 2
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Clipper: The Race Of Their Lives - Episode 4
programme.desc: Today the Clipper Race crews are going to face up their biggest challenge in this epic circumnavigation of the planet: the Mighty Pacific Ocean. Its fearsome reputation is well deserved and it is not clear how everyone will cope. We meet two novice Mexican sailors who join for the ride of their lives; and a sailor testing her nerve, as the teams are pushed to their limits, facing hurricane force winds, monstruous waves and relentless freezing temperatures.

programme.title: VOR Supremacy
programme.desc: The Volvo Ocean Race sails around 10 cities from all over the world, making it the ultimate test for sailors. Often described as the longest and toughest professional sporting event in the world, it is one of the sport’s big three events, alongside the Olympic Games and America’s Cup.The cannon blast in Alicante sends off the fleet, and once again shakes the sailing world. The veterans finally get their memory back while for the rookies it feels like mission impossible. They share the same obsession for the sea, and many of them have fully dedicated themselves for years, even decades trying to win it.The 2017-18 edition of the Volvo Ocean Race sets sail around the globe. The challenge is full on, and now they are all going rogue.

programme.title: VOR Identity
programme.desc: The Volvo Ocean Race visits 10 cities around the world making it the ultimate test for sailors around the world. Often described as the longest and toughest professional sporting event in the world, it is one of the sport’s Big Three events, alongside the Olympic Games and America’s Cup.However, to truly understand the race, it is better to think about the athletes who take part of it. Meet the teams and the superstars in Alicante on the eve of their quest to the Volvo Ocean Race. They share the same obsession for the sea, and many of them have fully dedicated themselves for years, even decades trying to win.Map the racecourse, get the latest intel on the boats and the tech and above all, find out what really moves the action of heroes from modern day offshore sailing. Watch it now, on Nautical Channel and become part of this sailing frenzy.

programme.title: Clipper: The Race Of Their Lives - Episode 4
programme.desc: Today the Clipper Race crews are going to face up their biggest challenge in this epic circumnavigation of the planet: the Mighty Pacific Ocean. Its fearsome reputation is well deserved and it is not clear how everyone will cope. We meet two novice Mexican sailors who join for the ride of their lives; and a sailor testing her nerve, as the teams are pushed to their limits, facing hurricane force winds, monstruous waves and relentless freezing temperatures.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 1
programme.desc: On this first episode of Inside Superyachts we meet with the Godfather of Superyacht builders – Feadship Yachts. Established in 1849, this Dutch shipyard is not only the number one in the industry but also the most famous of all superyacht builders, having created masterpieces for the likes of Steve Jobs.From the Netherlands we take a trip down to the ultra-discreet Principality of Monaco, host of the greatest yacht show in the world ‘The Monaco Yacht Show’. Then, we travel thousands of feet below the sea, exploring the vast ocean with Triton Submarines, utilized by some of films greatest names such as film director James Cameron and presenter David Attenborough.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 2
programme.desc: From the Godfather of builders to the Grandaddy of designers we visit the studio of Bannenberg & Rowell to unveil the legend that is the late great Jon Bannenberg. Following the winds of change we discuss the growing demand for explorer yachts. We meet London based H2 Design, and learn about the groundbreaking 107m explorer M/Y Ulysses.We continue our theme with EYOS Explorer and DAMEN yachts, the largest and most prolific ship builder on the planet.

programme.title: Facing Waves - Episode 7
programme.desc: Poland is a place of immense historical significant that goes back more than a thousand years. Although it is well known for its role in the Second World War, Poland is also being appreciated for its world class cuisine, breathtaking countryside and ease of travel. This week on Facing Waves, we are set to discover the beautiful waterways of Poland.

programme.title: Bol D'Or: The Swiss Do It Better
programme.desc: The Bol D’Or Mirabaud is the most important inland lake regatta in the world. Taking place in Switzerland, a landlocked nation that sits on top of the sailing world with multiple successes whether in the Olympic, Offshore or in Grand Prix Racing formats, and champion of two America’s Cup victories. The race started in 1939 and it is a symbolic race that brings together the best of Switzerland’s sailing tradition.Every year they welcome mono-hulls and multihulls on Lake Geneva, starting in the city of Geneva. More than 500 boats fight each other on this 123 kilometre (66.5 nautical miles) course from Geneva to Le Bouveret and back. It is a national treasure that every summer gathers thousands of spectators to the shores of Lake Geneva and really shows how the Swiss indeed do it better.Watch the race and become one with the beauty of Switzerland’s background, available on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: PaddleSUP Mission Amazon
programme.desc: Charlie Head will attempt the first ever stand-up paddle boarding of the main water source of the Amazon, the Rio Marañón in Peru. It’s never been attempted before and will be the most arduous and longest SUP journey ever made.Two months of using just his physical and mental strength, Charlie will overcome extreme terrain, trek around impassable river rock falls, survive freezing waters, high altitude, swarms of insects and suspicious local tribes.However, all the hardships will be worth it if he can help shine some light on one of the planet’s most endangered environments and educates us on conservation as over 20 new dams are planned to be constructed on the river's free-flowing stretches. This could have, according to scientists, drastic consequences on the Amazon basin forever.If Charlie does not embark on this adventure now, he may never be able to do so. Find out more with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 5
programme.desc: One of the many charms of the island of Vis lies in its 500 years long isolation as a military island. Vis has an unspoiled nature, magical beaches and the hospitality of its people is so authentic it will make you want to stay in this place forever. In this episode of Sail-Ho, we will be exploring this charming island.

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 6
programme.desc: Hello sailors! In this episode we will be island hopping the Zadar archipelago. We will visit the islands of Ugljan, Pašman and Pag, and our first stop will be the Ugljan, also known as the Olive Island. This is one of the most picturesque islands of the entire archipelago, so it will be a great start.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 3
programme.desc: Speed is the name of the game in this episode as we visit Dutch shipyard Heesen Yachts, renowned builder of record breaking, fast, lightweight aluminium superyachts. We talk to naval architects ‘Omega’ and we get an onboard look at the racing colour design of M/Y Aurelia, designed by Bannenberg & Rowell. Finally, no yacht would be complete without the latest technology and fastest onboard gadgets. We discover just what’s hot on a yacht with the most sought-after tech and AV firm, IdeaWorks.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 4
programme.desc: Italians are known for their quality and style, and their superyachts are no different. Undoubtedly the largest and most famous of Italian builders is Perini Navi, makers of one of the most ecologically friendly superyachts in the world, the Maltese Falcon. We meet with designer Ken Frievokh and engineer Damon Roberts to understand the technology behind their ground-breaking dyna-rig onboard the Maltese Falcon.We then take a look at some of the finest Italian boats built by one of the fastest growing shipyards: Mondomarine. We get an all access introduction to Viareggio’s Sealyon, whose opulent and glamorous interiors were designed by Martin Kemp at Candy & Candy.

programme.title: Brilliant Corners Haiti
programme.desc: The Caribbean is filled with places where tourism is rare or close to non-existent. However, there is so much potential for cultural and water sports tourism, they are called Brilliant corners. Nautical Channel is on a mission to explore the brilliant corners of the Caribbean.Going from Haiti all the way to Jamaica or Barbados, we will dive into these brilliant corners, many of them with no tourism at all. With a sustainable tourism approach, get ready to live a fully local experience on Brilliant Corners Haiti while we practice water sports, taste the traditional local dishes and talk with the habitants of the area.On this occasion, we venture into Haiti: the Caribbean country that shares the island of Hispaniola with the Dominican Republic. Many of Haiti’s landmarks date to the early 19th century, and despite still recovering from the earthquake of 2010, Haiti is a beautiful paradise for many visitors.Join Sam Bleakly, a travel writer, surfer, and a lecturer in cultural tourism, as he takes you under the skin of the coastline and inner parts of Haiti. Experience the Haitians history, art, culture, music, food, landscape, and their coastline as Sam leads you through what he calls, the off-beat track locations of the brilliant corners of the world.

programme.title: Heineken Regatta - Episode 1
programme.desc: The St. Maarten Heineken Regatta celebrates its 33th edition! This year, the Regatta Organization has overworked to design challenging courses and guarantee smooth sailing. Moreover, Heineken brings all the awesome music and a four-day party atmosphere considered unmissable by many. Relive the best moments of the 2013 Heineken Regatta!

programme.title: Heineken Regatta - Episode 1
programme.desc: Boasting world class competition and legendary parties, the St. Maarten Heineken Regatta reigns as the undisputed king of Caribbean sailing. Given the lineup of over 200 internationally acclaimed boats skippered by the sailing world's top athletes, the stakes are high and anything can happen.Sail with us across the highlights of the 34th edition of the Heineken Regatta!

programme.desc: Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli has advanced to the final of the Prada Cup against INEOS Team UK to decide who will be the final challenger for the 36th America's Cup against Emirates Team New Zealand. They will be competing in a first of 7 wins format. Watch out now this competition that has had thousands of spectators jumping off their seat, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: PaddleSUP Mission Amazon
programme.desc: Charlie Head will attempt the first ever stand-up paddle boarding of the main water source of the Amazon, the Rio Marañón in Peru. It’s never been attempted before and will be the most arduous and longest SUP journey ever made.Two months of using just his physical and mental strength, Charlie will overcome extreme terrain, trek around impassable river rock falls, survive freezing waters, high altitude, swarms of insects and suspicious local tribes.However, all the hardships will be worth it if he can help shine some light on one of the planet’s most endangered environments and educates us on conservation as over 20 new dams are planned to be constructed on the river's free-flowing stretches. This could have, according to scientists, drastic consequences on the Amazon basin forever.If Charlie does not embark on this adventure now, he may never be able to do so. Find out more with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Clipper: The Race Of Their Lives - Episode 4
programme.desc: Today the Clipper Race crews are going to face up their biggest challenge in this epic circumnavigation of the planet: the Mighty Pacific Ocean. Its fearsome reputation is well deserved and it is not clear how everyone will cope. We meet two novice Mexican sailors who join for the ride of their lives; and a sailor testing her nerve, as the teams are pushed to their limits, facing hurricane force winds, monstruous waves and relentless freezing temperatures.

programme.title: Surf Series: South Africa - Episode 1
programme.desc: South Africa has some of the best and most consistent surf breaks in the world but the qualifying series, for years, lacked events there. This year competitive surfing is back in South Africa and the season returned with a bang, with five QS Events ranging from 1 star, to 3 star, before culminating at the 6 star Ballito Pro. Let's reflect on the highlights of 2017.

programme.title: Locked In
programme.desc: Located just a few degrees south of the equator, Papua New Guinea is a tapestry of virgin rainforest, unique cultures and an expansive coastline. Pouring out of its ‘Nakanai Plateau’ is the Beriman River. Over 40km in length from source to sea, it drops more than 2,000m before reaching the Solomon Sea.The Beriman Gorge’s mysterious and towering canyon walls have never before been explored. Kayakers Ben Stookesberry, Chris Korbulic, Ben Marr and Pedro Oliva set out to conquer these previously unchartered whitewater rapids.This is one of kayaking’s most significant challenges and during the 13-day expedition, their skills and patience are pushed to their limits. They navigate twelve distinct gorges, numerous class-5 rapids, inescapable vertical walls, fluctuating water levels and dense jungle cover. All of these untamed natural obstacles stand in the way of the quartet’s successful first descent.

programme.title: Chef On Board - Episode 3
programme.desc: Lapo and his Captain are sailing to Pianosa, a very remote and exclusive Island. As it is a prison island, boats cannot come within a mile radius. This means Lapo is on a solo mission. Lapo will meet the prisoners who are keeping the island pristine but taking care of it’s natural fauna and flora. He will cook with ingredients from the prison garden and learn a secret dish from one of the wardens. Will this impress the Captain?

programme.title: Chef On Board - Episode 4
programme.desc: Lapo arrives on Giglio Island where he meets a local fisherman who supplies the restaurants with their seafood. On their trip out to sea, Lapo learns about species of the area and how to fish sustainably. He finds his catch when a bunch of girls who come from America come to dine on board. Now, its the right time to remember everything he learnt from the Maestros.

It’s never been attempted before and will be the most arduous and longest SUP journey ever made.Two months of using just his physical and mental strength, Charlie will overcome extreme terrain, trek around impassable river rock falls, survive freezing waters, high altitude, swarms of insects and suspicious local tribes.However, all the hardships will be worth it if he can help shine some light on one of the planet’s most endangered environments and educates us on conservation as over 20 new dams are planned to be constructed on the river's free-flowing stretches. This could have, according to scientists, drastic consequences on the Amazon basin forever.If Charlie does not embark on this adventure now, he may never be able to do so. Find out more with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 3
programme.desc: Speed is the name of the game in this episode as we visit Dutch shipyard Heesen Yachts, renowned builder of record breaking, fast, lightweight aluminium superyachts. We talk to naval architects ‘Omega’ and we get an onboard look at the racing colour design of M/Y Aurelia, designed by Bannenberg & Rowell. Finally, no yacht would be complete without the latest technology and fastest onboard gadgets. We discover just what’s hot on a yacht with the most sought-after tech and AV firm, IdeaWorks.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 4
programme.desc: Italians are known for their quality and style, and their superyachts are no different. Undoubtedly the largest and most famous of Italian builders is Perini Navi, makers of one of the most ecologically friendly superyachts in the world, the Maltese Falcon. We meet with designer Ken Frievokh and engineer Damon Roberts to understand the technology behind their ground-breaking dyna-rig onboard the Maltese Falcon.We then take a look at some of the finest Italian boats built by one of the fastest growing shipyards: Mondomarine. We get an all access introduction to Viareggio’s Sealyon, whose opulent and glamorous interiors were designed by Martin Kemp at Candy & Candy.

programme.title: International Surfing Association World Championship - World Junior Surfing Championship
programme.desc: This year’s 2017 ISA World Junior Surfing Championship will be held in Hyuga, Japan between the months of September and October. The championship will see junior athletes surfing as they compete not only to represent their home countries during the championship, but also as an opportunity to be chosen for the 2020 Olympics!

programme.title: International Surfing Association World Championship - World Surfing Games
programme.desc: Up to 200 surfers from 40 nations will battle for a medal in the ISA World Surfing Games 2017. Biarritz La Grande Plage will host the event, thus celebrating the 60th anniversary of surfing’s arrival in France. The athletes will compete for the titles: Men’s Individual, Women’s Individual, National World Champion, and Relay World Champion. Join us and have a taste of the high-performance surfing to come in Tokyo 2020!

programme.title: International Surfing Association World Championship - World Longboard Surfing Championship
programme.desc: An acclaimed competition thanks to the singularity of the longboard, this year’s 2018 ISA World Longboard Championship is back in the races. This year the competition will see 71 athletes from 22 nations surfing for the podium. And thanks to this year’s high-end location in Wanning, China, the championship will be even more exciting. Join us as we watch the best moments of this anticipated event!

programme.title: Kitesurf Odyssey 2017 - 2017
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to season 5 of The Boat Show, the meeting point for boat lovers and boating fans! Yachts are fascinating and complex, and their quality and value depend on many factors. Let's dive into the season with the Absolute 52 Fly before trying out the Ice Cat 61 and the Selva 900 S Line. Let's get started!

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 2
programme.desc: Are you ready for more salt water in this episode of The Boat Show? Today we are getting on board the Absolute Navetta 48, a 50 plus ft yacht with superb features. Then, we get onboard the Idea 58 WA before boarding the Fjord 44, one of the boats that gave rise to a new yacht model. Let's discover more!

programme.title: Behind The Scenes - Karujet World Championship
programme.desc: The much awaited 19th edition of the Karujet has finally roared through the island of Guadeloupe. With the world's top guns of Jet Ski racing on the pitch, these daredevils ploughed through waves at speeds of up to 80 knots.To bring this event to life, people from all over the world join to create what is becoming the biggest nautical event for Jet Ski lovers of the year. As competitors speed across the ocean, firm land spectators await in an exciting celebration. Who will become this year’s champion? Watch as Nautical Channel goes behind the scenes to interview the crazy brave competitors as we explore what it takes to fuel such a big event in the Caribbean island of Guadeloupe.

We meet two novice Mexican sailors who join for the ride of their lives; and a sailor testing her nerve, as the teams are pushed to their limits, facing hurricane force winds, monstruous waves and relentless freezing temperatures.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 3
programme.desc: Speed is the name of the game in this episode as we visit Dutch shipyard Heesen Yachts, renowned builder of record breaking, fast, lightweight aluminium superyachts. We talk to naval architects ‘Omega’ and we get an onboard look at the racing colour design of M/Y Aurelia, designed by Bannenberg & Rowell. Finally, no yacht would be complete without the latest technology and fastest onboard gadgets. We discover just what’s hot on a yacht with the most sought-after tech and AV firm, IdeaWorks.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 4
programme.desc: Italians are known for their quality and style, and their superyachts are no different. Undoubtedly the largest and most famous of Italian builders is Perini Navi, makers of one of the most ecologically friendly superyachts in the world, the Maltese Falcon. We meet with designer Ken Frievokh and engineer Damon Roberts to understand the technology behind their ground-breaking dyna-rig onboard the Maltese Falcon.We then take a look at some of the finest Italian boats built by one of the fastest growing shipyards: Mondomarine. We get an all access introduction to Viareggio’s Sealyon, whose opulent and glamorous interiors were designed by Martin Kemp at Candy & Candy.

programme.title: International Surfing Association World Championship - World Longboard Surfing Championship

programme.title: Locked In
programme.desc: Located just a few degrees south of the equator, Papua New Guinea is a tapestry of virgin rainforest, unique cultures and an expansive coastline. Pouring out of its ‘Nakanai Plateau’ is the Beriman River. Over 40km in length from source to sea, it drops more than 2,000m before reaching the Solomon Sea.The Beriman Gorge’s mysterious and towering canyon walls have never before been explored. Kayakers Ben Stookesberry, Chris Korbulic, Ben Marr and Pedro Oliva set out to conquer these previously unchartered whitewater rapids.This is one of kayaking’s most significant challenges and during the 13-day expedition, their skills and patience are pushed to their limits. They navigate twelve distinct gorges, numerous class-5 rapids, inescapable vertical walls, fluctuating water levels and dense jungle cover. All of these untamed natural obstacles stand in the way of the quartet’s successful first descent.

programme.title: PRADA Cup - Final Week 1
programme.desc: Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli has advanced to the final of the Prada Cup against INEOS Team UK to decide who will be the final challenger for the 36th America's Cup against Emirates Team New Zealand. They will be competing in a first of 7 wins format. Watch out now this competition that has had thousands of spectators jumping off their seat, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: P1 USA AquaX - Episode 3 - Review
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 3
programme.desc: This time we are going to focus on another exciting Olympic class, the RS:X. The RS:X was first introduced at the 2008 Olympic Games in China. Being the most affordable yachting class eligible for competition in the Olympics, it has been taken up by a large number of sailors from more than 60 nations over 6 continents competing at numerous regattas worldwide.Stay tuned for the next episode as we follow the sailing action around the world! The final countdown for the Olympics in Tokyo has only just begun!

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 4
programme.desc: Welcome to the fourth episode of Sailing to Tokyo 2020, in this month’s episode we will focus on two classes that we will be introduced to the Olympics for Paris 2024. iQFOiL and KiteSurfing are set to make their Summer Olympic debut in Marseille after being approved as two out of the ten sailing events on the programme.Vanora ENGADINWIND by Dakine, is one of the most famous water sport events in the world and at this windsurfing festival various events take place, including some freestyle action. Held annually during August on Lake Silvaplana, it attracts some of the best athletes the water sport world has to showcase. Let's dive into the action!

programme.title: Clipper: The Race Of Their Lives - Episode 5
programme.desc: In tonight's episode the Clipper Race fleet takes on the Asia Pacific Leg, split across two stages with spectacular destinations in China; crews celebrate the Chinese New Year at sea and swelter in the backing tropical heat; there's a second visit by King Neptune as father and son cross the equator and loved ones missed on Valentines Day.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 1 - Origin
programme.desc: From under the Golden Gate Bridge down to the Santa Cruz Islands the three friends and their little boat are now heading south. However bad weather is on the horizon and signs that the film and the filmmaker are already thinking about abandoning ship are starting to leak through. After a long night of sea sickness, it’s time to face reality and see who’s going to stick around and who’s going to go.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 2 - Reality
programme.desc: Now closing in on Mexico and having spent nearly a month at sea, the crew are facing some realities they didn’t expect. It’s cold, it’s wet, and its nothing like what they were hoping. Adding to the chaos, Barry is emotionally lost and opens up about what he left behind to go on this trip. As the crew pokes around Catalina Island and down into Mexico a reckoning with what the trip is and what they wanted it to be is on the horizon and with it a new reality of life.

programme.title: Locked In
programme.desc: Located just a few degrees south of the equator, Papua New Guinea is a tapestry of virgin rainforest, unique cultures and an expansive coastline. Pouring out of its ‘Nakanai Plateau’ is the Beriman River. They navigate twelve distinct gorges, numerous class-5 rapids, inescapable vertical walls, fluctuating water levels and dense jungle cover. All of these untamed natural obstacles stand in the way of the quartet’s successful first descent.

programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 7
programme.desc: In this new episode of Sail-Ho we are visiting Dugi Otok, the longest and biggest island of the Zadar archipelago. It is also the 2nd most indented island in all of the Adriatic Sea, with gorgeous beaches and beautiful nature we believe is worth exploring completely. Come join us in this new adventure!

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 8
programme.desc: Welcome to Sail-Ho Croatia! We're in Rab, the island known by many as the island of love and happiness since Roman times. It has more than 30 sandy beaches, 2500 hours of sunshine and a mouthwatering gastronomy. Come join us and find out why this place is considered a happy place by so many!

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 5
programme.desc: Arguably, a superyacht is the most glamorous holiday you can take. Burgess yachts, the largest yacht broker in the world, talks to us about the process of owning and/or chartering your very own yacht.We take a look at just how yachts are marketed and meet with Australian Shipbuilders Silver Yachts, who achieved the amazing feat of getting a Mercedes car onto their Yacht Silver Fast for one of the most epic yachts commercials ever.For those wanting more than a charter, but don’t want to own a boat outright, we introduce them to MAHA Yachts, a brand new concept bridging the gap between Charter and Sales – a unique collaboration with the world leading hotelier Adrian Zecha from Aman Hotels and Feadship.We sign off with one last adventure underwater, exploring the sea with Deep Flight and Rainbow Fish, interviewing Dr. Xin Wu who is helping to pioneer deep water tourism.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 6
programme.desc: In the latest episode of Inside Superyachts we interview Ken Freivokh, designer of some of the world’s most iconic yachts and concepts ever. Besides talking to the genius behind some of the best yacht masterpieces we meet with Thirty C leading CGI concept designers.Lastly, we know that no yacht would be complete without a vessel to get you to and from shore. Pascoe Tenders open their doors to us to have a look inside these space-aged, hyperluxurious tenders.

After the meeting in Lanzarote, some of the best windsurfers in the world meet again in Cadiz to compete in the International Games IQ Foil Cadiz. Enjoy the competition in Cadiz!

This year the competition will see 71 athletes from 22 nations surfing for the podium. And thanks to this year’s high-end location in Wanning, China, the championship will be even more exciting. programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Clipper: The Race Of Their Lives - Episode 5
programme.desc: In tonight's episode the Clipper Race fleet takes on the Asia Pacific Leg, split across two stages with spectacular destinations in China; crews celebrate the Chinese New Year at sea and swelter in the backing tropical heat; there's a second visit by King Neptune as father and son cross the equator and loved ones missed on Valentines Day.

programme.title: Locked In
programme.desc: Located just a few degrees south of the equator, Papua New Guinea is a tapestry of virgin rainforest, unique cultures and an expansive coastline. Pouring out of its ‘Nakanai Plateau’ is the Beriman River. Over 40km in length from source to sea, it drops more than 2,000m before reaching the Solomon Sea.The Beriman Gorge’s mysterious and towering canyon walls have never before been explored. Kayakers Ben Stookesberry, Chris Korbulic, Ben Marr and Pedro Oliva set out to conquer these previously unchartered whitewater rapids.This is one of kayaking’s most significant challenges and during the 13-day expedition, their skills and patience are pushed to their limits. They navigate twelve distinct gorges, numerous class-5 rapids, inescapable vertical walls, fluctuating water levels and dense jungle cover. All of these untamed natural obstacles stand in the way of the quartet’s successful first descent.

programme.title: Heineken Regatta - Episode 1
programme.desc: Get ready to rumble in Simpson Bay where the St. Maarten Heineken Regatta will celebrate its 35th anniversary edition! With the official slogan being Serious Fun, it is no wonder people come from over 25 countries to attend this one of a kind regatta. Given this year's lineup, we are expecting some of the best sailing in St. Maarten. Watch the highlights of the 2015 edition!

programme.title: Heineken Regatta - Episode 1
programme.desc: The St. Maarten Heineken Regatta is about to celebrate its 36th edition. This world renowned sailing event offers four days of World Class Racing, with trade winds coming from the North East, an average temperature of 25°C and crystal clear waters. Courses will take participants along coastal races, as well as around the island, presenting the beautiful beaches, rock formations and other scenery. On shore, participants and visitors will be able to enjoy the culture, cuisine and music from both the Dutch and French side. Are you going to miss out? Come dive in at the 2016 Heineken Regatta!

programme.title: A Date with the Strait
programme.desc: The Bass Strait is a strip of water that divides the Australian mainland from Tasmania. Knowing that wild storms make the strait twice as rough as the English Channel, sailors stand in awe of the strong currents.In a death-defying act, two Aussies and a young Californian kneeling on their boards brave the 300 km/186.5mi paddle across the Bass Strait. Their adventure lasts several days with stops on small islands along the way. Apart from sharks, currents and threatening waves, win-or-bust Zeb Walsh, Brad Gaul and Jack Bark face sickness, sore muscles and dehydration. Follow their journey on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Rediscover North America
programme.desc: Six friends decide to cross 12 states on their canoe, embarking on the adventure of a lifetime. From the Gulf of Mexico to the Arctic Ocean, they battled against the current on the Mighty Mississippi, weathered ice and snow, crossed massive lakes, dodged shifting forest fires and shot challenging whitewater, all in a blasting total of 5200 miles. After eight long months, the expedition is ending, but with only 3 canoes, limited provisions and each other’s company along the way, will they be able to make it in one piece? Find it out on Nautical Channel. Awards: Best Adventure Travel Film – Reel Paddling Film Festival 2017. Winner Best Adventure Film – Waterwalker Film Festival 2017. Winner Canoe and Kayak Magazine’s 2016 Expedition of the Year Award.

programme.title: Chef On Board - Episode 5
programme.desc: Lapo is back on land for the Surf and Turf Season finale. He will be meeting the Tuscan Cowboys to learn about Tuscany’s beef industry as well as taking to the waters with a local fisherman to fish for sea urchins. On this final episode, Lapo is ready to give his all and prove himself worthy of his captain's approval. Will the Captain finally soften to Lapo?

programme.title: Cannes Yachting Festival - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to the sunny French Riviera for the 2016 Cannes Yachting Festival, a gathering in the name of luxury and excellence. In this year's edition, more than 200 models will be shown for the first time ever in the world. Let's find out the highlights of this incredible boat show.

programme.title: International Surfing Association World Championship - World Longboard Surfing Championship
programme.desc: An acclaimed competition thanks to the singularity of the longboard, this year’s 2018 ISA World Longboard Championship is back in the races. This year the competition will see 71 athletes from 22 nations surfing for the podium. And thanks to this year’s high-end location in Wanning, China, the championship will be even more exciting. Join us as we watch the best moments of this anticipated event!

programme.title: Kitesurf Odyssey 2017 - 2017
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 5
programme.desc: Arguably, a superyacht is the most glamorous holiday you can take. Burgess yachts, the largest yacht broker in the world, talks to us about the process of owning and/or chartering your very own yacht.We take a look at just how yachts are marketed and meet with Australian Shipbuilders Silver Yachts, who achieved the amazing feat of getting a Mercedes car onto their Yacht Silver Fast for one of the most epic yachts commercials ever.For those wanting more than a charter, but don’t want to own a boat outright, we introduce them to MAHA Yachts, a brand new concept bridging the gap between Charter and Sales – a unique collaboration with the world leading hotelier Adrian Zecha from Aman Hotels and Feadship.We sign off with one last adventure underwater, exploring the sea with Deep Flight and Rainbow Fish, interviewing Dr. Xin Wu who is helping to pioneer deep water tourism.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 6
programme.desc: In the latest episode of Inside Superyachts we interview Ken Freivokh, designer of some of the world’s most iconic yachts and concepts ever. Besides talking to the genius behind some of the best yacht masterpieces we meet with Thirty C leading CGI concept designers.Lastly, we know that no yacht would be complete without a vessel to get you to and from shore. Pascoe Tenders open their doors to us to have a look inside these space-aged, hyperluxurious tenders.

programme.title: P1 USA AquaX - Episode 3 - Review
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: PRADA Cup - Final Week 2
programme.desc: We are coming to an end in this Prada Cup. So far, Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli has been winning the 2021 PRADA's Cup Final, however, every sailing enthusiast knows winds can quickly change any moment. Will INEOS Team UK take advantage of the situation? Or is this already a clear victory for Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli? Find out now, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: IQFOiL International Games - Cádiz
programme.desc: More than 150 athletes from 18 different countries competed in Cadiz for one of the most coveted titles of this class: the second stop of the International Circuit of the IQ Foil class. After the meeting in Lanzarote, some of the best windsurfers in the world meet again in Cadiz to compete in the International Games IQ Foil Cadiz. Enjoy the competition in Cadiz!

programme.title: Rolex World Sailing Awards - Episode 1
programme.desc: 2021 has marked a great year in the nautical world. With the Olympics, America's Cup and Vendee Globe, it is great sailing is back and strong. Let's look back on the past year and find out who of this year's nominees will become World Sailor of the Year as well as what sustainable project has been received the World Sailing 11th Hour Racing Sustainability Award.

programme.title: Rediscover North America
programme.desc: Six friends decide to cross 12 states on their canoe, embarking on the adventure of a lifetime. From the Gulf of Mexico to the Arctic Ocean, they battled against the current on the Mighty Mississippi, weathered ice and snow, crossed massive lakes, dodged shifting forest fires and shot challenging whitewater, all in a blasting total of 5200 miles. After eight long months, the expedition is ending, but with only 3 canoes, limited provisions and each other’s company along the way, will they be able to make it in one piece? Find it out on Nautical Channel. Awards: Best Adventure Travel Film – Reel Paddling Film Festival 2017. Winner Best Adventure Film – Waterwalker Film Festival 2017. Winner Canoe and Kayak Magazine’s 2016 Expedition of the Year Award.

programme.title: Clipper: The Race Of Their Lives - Episode 5
programme.desc: In tonight's episode the Clipper Race fleet takes on the Asia Pacific Leg, split across two stages with spectacular destinations in China; crews celebrate the Chinese New Year at sea and swelter in the backing tropical heat; there's a second visit by King Neptune as father and son cross the equator and loved ones missed on Valentines Day.

programme.title: Rolex World Sailing Awards - Episode 1

Let's look back on the past year and find out who of this year's nominees will become World Sailor of the Year as well as what sustainable project has been received the World Sailing 11th Hour Racing Sustainability Award.

programme.title: San Borondón
programme.desc: The popular legend claims San Borondón is an island that appears and disappears, normally under a heavy veil of fog. As adventurers departed on missions to be the first to locate it, not all returned from the depths of the ocean.Now, San Borondón is the name many local and international surfers have given to a dangerous wave spot on the Canary Islands. The film producer Rayco Cano, his team and five big wave riders embrace us in their story about these huge waves. The film features windsurfing pro Daniel Bruch, German big wave surfer Alexander Zirke, Manuel Lezcano, Ayoze Fernandea and David Rodal Santiago Vilayta.San Borondón stands for big wave surfing and adrenalin driven surfers, who risk a lot on their surfing boards and SUPs. The jet ski is a necessary safety tool. Discover hidden places with beautiful nature in the Canary Islands as this team of adventurers dive into action on the water, available on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Karujet 2017
programme.desc: The much awaited 20th edition of the Karujet has finally roared through the island of Guadeloupe. With the world's top guns of Jet Ski racing on the pitch, these daredevils ploughed through waves at speeds of up to 80 knots. Competition is rough as some participants break down and return to the beach with broken bones, others just give up, while the remaining brave riders fight to the end!The event takes place in no other than the festive and colorful Caribbean island, Guadeloupe. To bring this event to life, people from all over the world join to create what is becoming the biggest nautical event for Jet Ski lovers of the year.As competitors speed across the ocean, firm land spectators await in an exciting celebration. Who will become this year’s champion? Join Nautical channel as we follow the 20th edition of what might be the last Karujet.

programme.title: International Surfing Association World Championship - World Adaptive Surfing Championship
programme.desc: This year’s anticipated 2017 ISA World Adaptive Surfing Championship will be held in sunny San Diego in the renown La Jolla neighborhood. The physically challenged surfers participate in teams of up to eight members, and represent their home countries as they race through the waters. With various events and a large following, the Championship is a fun and unique experience; both for the surfers and its spectators.

programme.title: Alacati Windsurf Mecca
programme.desc: Alacati is a town on Turkey’s Cesme Peninsula and for many a Windsurf Mecca. The town is famous for its constant winds and drives the attention of people who like practicing wind sports such as Windsurfing, Kitesurfing, SUP and many more. For years now, the small town is experiencing the impact of windsurfing in their economy, society and lifestyle as it attracts the attention of people worldwide that prefer the Turkish coast to practice water sports.Experience the Alacati Windsurf Mecca that will be sure to blow your mind on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 3
programme.desc: Nowadays we focus on technology for boat management and driving systems, and the Xenta Pershing 5X has both. After trying in the water this boat, we move on to discover the Karnic SL800 and the Grand Soleil 52 Long Cruise in Cala Galera. Come join us in today's episode!

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 4
programme.desc: In The Boat Show today we will see a large pleasure craft from Italy equipped with a Swedish propulsion system. We are talking about the Amer 110! Then, we try out in the water the Marshall M2 and discover the Bruce 22, an electric boat that is mesmerizing people from all over the world.

programme.title: Jet RAID - Spain
programme.desc: It's summer and the spirits are high in the second round of the Jet Raid 2019 in Spain. After visiting Greece, the series are going to La Manga, not far from Murcia. We are expecting the best action from the 25 riders that continue in the race: Jean-Bruno Pastorello is the favourite as he won in Greece, but we also have some other serious challengers like Alain Martin, David Chassier or Sébastien Biondi.

Burgess yachts, the largest yacht broker in the world, talks to us about the process of owning and/or chartering your very own yacht.We take a look at just how yachts are marketed and meet with Australian Shipbuilders Silver Yachts, who achieved the amazing feat of getting a Mercedes car onto their Yacht Silver Fast for one of the most epic yachts commercials ever.For those wanting more than a charter, but don’t want to own a boat outright, we introduce them to MAHA Yachts, a brand new concept bridging the gap between Charter and Sales – a unique collaboration with the world leading hotelier Adrian Zecha from Aman Hotels and Feadship.We sign off with one last adventure underwater, exploring the sea with Deep Flight and Rainbow Fish, interviewing Dr. Xin Wu who is helping to pioneer deep water tourism.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 6
programme.desc: In the latest episode of Inside Superyachts we interview Ken Freivokh, designer of some of the world’s most iconic yachts and concepts ever. Besides talking to the genius behind some of the best yacht masterpieces we meet with Thirty C leading CGI concept designers.Lastly, we know that no yacht would be complete without a vessel to get you to and from shore. Pascoe Tenders open their doors to us to have a look inside these space-aged, hyperluxurious tenders.

programme.title: International Surfing Association World Championship - World Adaptive Surfing Championship
programme.desc: This year’s anticipated 2017 ISA World Adaptive Surfing Championship will be held in sunny San Diego in the renown La Jolla neighborhood. The physically challenged surfers participate in teams of up to eight members, and represent their home countries as they race through the waters. With various events and a large following, the Championship is a fun and unique experience; both for the surfers and its spectators.

programme.title: Alacati Windsurf Mecca
programme.desc: Alacati is a town on Turkey’s Cesme Peninsula and for many a Windsurf Mecca. The town is famous for its constant winds and drives the attention of people who like practicing wind sports such as Windsurfing, Kitesurfing, SUP and many more. For years now, the small town is experiencing the impact of windsurfing in their economy, society and lifestyle as it attracts the attention of people worldwide that prefer the Turkish coast to practice water sports.Experience the Alacati Windsurf Mecca that will be sure to blow your mind on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: PRADA Cup - Final Week 2
programme.desc: We are coming to an end in this Prada Cup. So far, Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli has been winning the 2021 PRADA's Cup Final, however, every sailing enthusiast knows winds can quickly change any moment. Will INEOS Team UK take advantage of the situation? Or is this already a clear victory for Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli? Find out now, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Inside Superyachts - Episode 5
programme.desc: Arguably, a superyacht is the most glamorous holiday you can take. Pascoe Tenders open their doors to us to have a look inside these space-aged, hyperluxurious tenders.

programme.title: Jet RAID - Spain
programme.desc: It's summer and the spirits are high in the second round of the Jet Raid 2019 in Spain. After visiting Greece, the series are going to La Manga, not far from Murcia. We are expecting the best action from the 25 riders that continue in the race: Jean-Bruno Pastorello is the favourite as he won in Greece, but we also have some other serious challengers like Alain Martin, David Chassier or Sébastien Biondi.

programme.title: H2O Racing: F1H2O World Championship - Episode 7 - GP of Zhengzhou - China
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 5
programme.desc: Hello and welcome to the fifth episode of Sailing to Tokyo 2020! We continue with more sailing action from the current fastest Olympic sailing classes, in Lake Attersee, Austria for the 2020 Forward WIP 49er, 49er FX & Nacra 17 European Championships. After many cancellations due to the Covid pandemic this event was the most important for these Olympic classes in 2020.Get on board with us as we go Sailing to Tokyo!

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 6
programme.desc: To end the season we focus on one class that will be introduced for Paris 2024. Kiteboarding is set to make its summer Olympic debut in Marseille after being approved as two of the then sailing events on the programme. We will also head to Italy's Genoa to focus on the L30 class, which is a candidate to become an Olympic class in Paris 2024. In this majestic Italian city, the 2020 EUROSAF Mixed Offshore European Championships took place with some of the most legendary sailors participating. Let's get going!

programme.title: Clipper: The Race Of Their Lives - Episode 6
programme.desc: Coming up we are in the US coast, where the crews take on the Pacific from Seattle down to Panama; the fleet transits through one of the modern wonders of the world - the Panama Canal; and finally a testing shorter race through the Caribbean up to the big apple, as the teams converge on New York.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 3 - Freedom
programme.desc: Deeper south into the Baja Peninsula, the weather has calm and sunny days are more common. As we open the episode with the boat breaking free from being trapped by an anchor locked in the reef, there is a new sense of freedom. On the horizon are rare and never-before seen islands with beached seals, isolated lighthouse keepers, and massive salt mines. As they discover the new and uncharted, word comes in that surf is coming and now the race is on to get the best location to capture the incoming waves.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 4 - Discovery
programme.desc: Deep into the empty desert of Mexico’s Baja Peninsula, the three are now in pursuit of a storm swell and predictions of surf. As the head towards what’s to be an iconic moment in the trip the stop off in Tortuga Bay and have a peculiar discovery of fossilized shark’s teeth deep in the heart of this cake yellow desert. Charging south on the heals of incoming surf they land in the midst of reach surf culture and smack in the middle of society. Now after over a month in isolation the three wrestle with the experience while chasing after the best waves of the trip.

programme.title: Rediscover North America
programme.desc: Six friends decide to cross 12 states on their canoe, embarking on the adventure of a lifetime. From the Gulf of Mexico to the Arctic Ocean, they battled against the current on the Mighty Mississippi, weathered ice and snow, crossed massive lakes, dodged shifting forest fires and shot challenging whitewater, all in a blasting total of 5200 miles. After eight long months, the expedition is ending, but with only 3 canoes, limited provisions and each other’s company along the way, will they be able to make it in one piece? Find it out on Nautical Channel. Awards: Best Adventure Travel Film – Reel Paddling Film Festival 2017. Winner Best Adventure Film – Waterwalker Film Festival 2017. Winner Canoe and Kayak Magazine’s 2016 Expedition of the Year Award.

programme.title: IQFOiL International Games - Cádiz
programme.desc: More than 150 athletes from 18 different countries competed in Cadiz for one of the most coveted titles of this class: the second stop of the International Circuit of the IQ Foil class. After the meeting in Lanzarote, some of the best windsurfers in the world meet again in Cadiz to compete in the International Games IQ Foil Cadiz. Enjoy the competition in Cadiz!

programme.title: Rolex World Sailing Awards - Episode 1
programme.desc: 2021 has marked a great year in the nautical world. With the Olympics, America's Cup and Vendee Globe, it is great sailing is back and strong. Let's look back on the past year and find out who of this year's nominees will become World Sailor of the Year as well as what sustainable project has been received the World Sailing 11th Hour Racing Sustainability Award.

programme.title: Rolex Spirit Of Yachting - Episode 1 - SailGP
programme.desc: Rolex Spirit of Yachting 2022 has started with a big bang: we are in the Australia Sail Grand Prix about to live the magic of the end of Season 2 in SailGP. Crews fight for the first spot in their flying catamarans, reaching speeds of up to 100 km/h. A single mistake means staying out of competition, and with 1 million euros at stake, everyone wants to give their all.

Find it out on Nautical Channel. Awards: Best Adventure Travel Film – Reel Paddling Film Festival 2017. Winner Best Adventure Film – Waterwalker Film Festival 2017. Winner Canoe and Kayak Magazine’s 2016 Expedition of the Year Award.

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 9
programme.desc: Welcome back sailors! Today we are indulging in the most fragrant gardens of the islands of Cres and Losinj where the beauty of nature is next level. These islands are also known as the favourite place for many boaters who like to enjoy beautiful beaches and top restaurants. Let's dive in!

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 10
programme.desc: In this episode we are in the Opatija Riviera - the jewel box of the Northern Adriatic. With its enchanting architecture and romantic seaside promenades, it instantly takes us back to glorious summer days of the historical elite and royals. Now, let us take you here in Sail-Ho Croatia!

programme.title: Discovering Rhodes - Part 1
programme.desc: In this episode we will visit the old town and the palace of the grand master, before we refresh ourselves in the Kallithea Springs, and discover why Rhodes is considered as the sailing Mecca of the Dodecanese. We’ll take a trip to the beautiful Stegna beach to dive in the immaculate crystal blue waters, before venturing out on a SUP, a stand-up paddle board, to experience the unique and ever-growing practice of SUP Yoga. Finally, we’ll visit Panagia Tsambika, a hilltop monastery considered by some to be miracle-working.

programme.title: Discovering Rhodes - Part 2
programme.desc: In this second episode, we will visit the historic and picturesque Mandraki Harbour before exploring the natural attractions of Butterfly Valley and Seven Springs. Rhodes is often known as the paradise island for windsurfers, so we will not miss the chance to see how it feels to be on a board out on the open water. Finally, we will visit the must-see archaeological sites that aren’t to be missed before leaving the island, the town and the Acropolis of Lindos.

programme.title: Un día en...Salón Internacional Náutico de Barcelona - Salón Internacional Barcelona - Ep3
programme.desc: We invite you to discover and remember the best moments in "A day at the Barcelona International Boat Show".During the 4 days of the exhibition, you can discover more than 275 international exhibitors and almost 700 boats on the water, and many more boats at the show.

programme.title: Hempel World Cup Series - Palma de Mallorca
programme.desc: Welcome to the 51st Trofeo Princesa Sofía regatta in Palma de Mallorca! More than a thousand of accomplished sailing athletes compete in the first Hempel World Cup Series regatta of the new Olympic cycle. As the start of the European racing season, a great sense of urgency and enthusiasm can be felt among competitors, and as a vital event, we are eager to see how their training has gone over the winter.

programme.title: Hempel World Cup Series - Medemblik
programme.desc: The Hempel Sailing World Cup Series is back. The 2021 season takes place in Medemblik, The Netherlands, during the famous Allianz Regatta. This is the first Hempel Cup Series since the outbreak of the COVID-19 virus, and sailors are excited.A grand total of 350 sailors from 47 different countries are participating across eight Olympic classes. Watch the best moments of the competition at The Netherlands.

programme.title: Bass By Kayak
programme.desc: Bass by Kayak follows a small expedition party formed by four adventurers crossing from the Australian mainland to Tasmania in sea kayaks. With a 300 km separation, a bare four hundred people have made the crossing of the Bass Strait in the modern era. Before then, Aboriginal Australians sailed in their canoes the shocking distance during the last ice age.This adventure makes strategic stops on the islands along the way; however, it is one of the toughest and deadliest journeys in the world. Adventure filmmaker Beau Miles narrates the expedition as the first ever feature film of the crossing via sea kayaks.The travellers will experience chafe, storms, bad cooking and an emerging mate-ship between men that barely know each other.Watch it now with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Cannes Yachting Festival - Episode 1
programme.desc: The glamorous city of Cannes hosts the luxury Yachting Festival par excellence for the 40th time this year and a new Luxury Gallery has been opened in the Palais de Festivals to celebrate this landmark anniversary. Breathe in the boat show’s opulence and learn about the latest trends in the yachting world!

programme.title: Cannes Yachting Festival - Episode 1
programme.desc: Since 1977, the Cannes Yachting Festival takes place every September in elegant, luxurious surroundings in the sparkling bay of Cannes. We are ready for the 2018 edition that promises the latest trends in the nautical industry. Jump on board the 2018 Cannes Yachting Festival to discover superyachts, equipment, regattas, competitions and more.

programme.title: Discovering Rhodes - Part 2
programme.desc: In this second episode, we will visit the historic and picturesque Mandraki Harbour before exploring the natural attractions of Butterfly Valley and Seven Springs. Rhodes is often known as the paradise island for windsurfers, so we will not miss the chance to see how it feels to be on a board out on the open water. Finally, we will visit the must-see archaeological sites that aren’t to be missed before leaving the island, the town and the Acropolis of Lindos.

programme.desc: It's summer and the spirits are high in the second round of the Jet Raid 2019 in Spain. After visiting Greece, the series are going to La Manga, not far from Murcia. We are expecting the best action from the 25 riders that continue in the race: Jean-Bruno Pastorello is the favourite as he won in Greece, but we also have some other serious challengers like Alain Martin, David Chassier or Sébastien Biondi.

programme.title: Bass By Kayak
programme.desc: Bass by Kayak follows a small expedition party formed by four adventurers crossing from the Australian mainland to Tasmania in sea kayaks. With a 300 km separation, a bare four hundred people have made the crossing of the Bass Strait in the modern era. Before then, Aboriginal Australians sailed in their canoes the shocking distance during the last ice age.This adventure makes strategic stops on the islands along the way; however, it is one of the toughest and deadliest journeys in the world. Adventure filmmaker Beau Miles narrates the expedition as the first ever feature film of the crossing via sea kayaks.The travellers will experience chafe, storms, bad cooking and an emerging mate-ship between men that barely know each other.Watch it now with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Cape Verdean Kite Crossing
programme.desc: Mitu Monteiro, a Cape Verdean kitesurfer and multi-time world champion, attempts the solo crossing of the twelve volcanic islands of his native archipelago, never accomplished before.Taking on more than 730km of kitesurfing, interspersed with island traverses by only man-powered means totalling approximately 200km, Mitu’s challenges lay ahead of him. With a total trajectory of nearly 1,000km and a route that had to be thought and rethought due to wind direction, shark infested waters, and distances that push this athlete to his limit, sweat will flow and his nerves will surely be put to the test.Mitu’s maverick journey across marvellous Atlantic waters and beaches, through inhospitable lunar landscapes and the lush vegetation of the volcanic islands is truly a SURFARI on a kite. Relive the whole experience on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Barrel
programme.desc: Since the first wave was ridden, surfers have shared stories and experiences to try to explain the wonders inside the waves they ride.Professional and amateur surfers, photographers and videographers around the world have the possibility to display their passion: Barrel surfing. This term is used by surfers to describe riding well inside the curve of a finely shaped breaking wave, for many described as magical.The Barrel is all about surfer’s skills, creativity and love for adventure as they look for the best waves for tube riding. Get inside the barrel of the wave, with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 5
programme.desc: Built by one of the oldest boat yards, the Baglietto MV 19 is a classic yet modern boat. We will try it out first before boarding the Ranieri 220SH and trying on the water the Solaris 47, an evolution of the 58 and the 50 with features for a reduced crew. Let's jump onboard!

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 6
programme.desc: In today's episode of The Boat Show we start in Greece to test a boat built by BSK Marine, the BSK Marine Skipper 4X 90. Then, we jump on board the Solaris 42 a boat designed by Soto Acebal. Finally, we try on the water the Capelli 50, a boat with almost 15 meters long and powered by four 425 horsepower outboards.

programme.title: H2O Racing: Aquabike World Championship - Episode 1 Season Preview
programme.desc: Welcome to the 25th season of the UIM ABP Aquabike World Championship. The world's best jetski racers are once again looking for the UIM ABP World title in six categories: the Ski Division GP1, the Ski Ladies GP1, the Freestyle, the Runabout GP1 as well as the Ski GP Junior 3.3 and 3.2.They will prove their skills across different Grand Prixes around the world. The rider who gets the most points throughout the season will be crowned world champion.Get to know what to expect from this year's edition in this season preview.

programme.title: Clipper: The Race Of Their Lives - Episode 7
programme.desc: After nearly a year of exciting sailing, the biennial Clipper Race is approaching its finale: the North Atlantic provides a tense final ocean crossing, with one team experiencing a major set back; we meet a crew member whose life has truly changed thanks to the race while the others reflect on this extraordinary experience. Also, there's an spectacular finish with the overall results going down to the wire.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 5 - Return
programme.desc: After nearly two months at sea, which now seems like lifetimes compounded by the suffering and challenge of the passage. The three are closing out their trip home and saving the best for last. Shocked by what seems to be death island, the three uncover shark hunters and in their last village before departure battle to resupply with the essentials of gas and water with the last pennies on the boat. Meanwhile the struggles of our filmmaker’s journey return along with the fear of going back to all that was left behind.

programme.title: Barcolana 50
programme.desc: For all sea and sailing lovers and those who would never miss this one-of-a-kind show, the place is always the same every year: Trieste. Founded in 1969 thanks to the initiative of the yacht club Società Velica di Barcola e Grignano, the Barcolana competition has won its worldwide renown.This year’s 50th edition will be packed with surprises: more than 400 offshore and onshore activities, the opportunity to admire the vessel Nave Vespucci, the Barcolana 50 special postage stamp, and the Frecce Tricolori airshow just a few minutes before the start of the race. Stunningly huge yachts and sailboats steered by sailing enthusiasts will compete in the Regatta to mark this special edition of Barcolana 50.Follow this year’s competition of Barcolana 50: the international regatta that has won the Guinness World Record for the greatest sailing race.

programme.title: Bass By Kayak
programme.desc: Bass by Kayak follows a small expedition party formed by four adventurers crossing from the Australian mainland to Tasmania in sea kayaks. programme.desc: Welcome to the 25th season of the UIM ABP Aquabike World Championship. The world's best jetski racers are once again looking for the UIM ABP World title in six categories: the Ski Division GP1, the Ski Ladies GP1, the Freestyle, the Runabout GP1 as well as the Ski GP Junior 3.3 and 3.2.They will prove their skills across different Grand Prixes around the world. The rider who gets the most points throughout the season will be crowned world champion.Get to know what to expect from this year's edition in this season preview.

programme.title: Under the Hull - Under the Hull
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Bass By Kayak
programme.desc: Bass by Kayak follows a small expedition party formed by four adventurers crossing from the Australian mainland to Tasmania in sea kayaks. With a 300 km separation, a bare four hundred people have made the crossing of the Bass Strait in the modern era. Before then, Aboriginal Australians sailed in their canoes the shocking distance during the last ice age.This adventure makes strategic stops on the islands along the way; however, it is one of the toughest and deadliest journeys in the world. Adventure filmmaker Beau Miles narrates the expedition as the first ever feature film of the crossing via sea kayaks.The travellers will experience chafe, storms, bad cooking and an emerging mate-ship between men that barely know each other.Watch it now with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Sailing to the Games - Episode 6
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Discovering Rhodes - Part 1
programme.desc: In this episode we will visit the old town and the palace of the grand master, before we refresh ourselves in the Kallithea Springs, and discover why Rhodes is considered as the sailing Mecca of the Dodecanese. We’ll take a trip to the beautiful Stegna beach to dive in the immaculate crystal blue waters, before venturing out on a SUP, a stand-up paddle board, to experience the unique and ever-growing practice of SUP Yoga. Finally, we’ll visit Panagia Tsambika, a hilltop monastery considered by some to be miracle-working.

programme.title: Discovering Rhodes - Part 2
programme.desc: In this second episode, we will visit the historic and picturesque Mandraki Harbour before exploring the natural attractions of Butterfly Valley and Seven Springs. Rhodes is often known as the paradise island for windsurfers, so we will not miss the chance to see how it feels to be on a board out on the open water. Finally, we will visit the must-see archaeological sites that aren’t to be missed before leaving the island, the town and the Acropolis of Lindos.

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 11
programme.desc: In this last episode of the season, we visit Zagreb, the capital of the country and one of the most popular destinations in Croatia. As a city with a rich history and an abundance of cultural events, it is the perfect place to include at the end of our Croatian adventure. Let's check it out!

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 1
programme.desc: Hello Sailors! It’s such a great pleasure to welcome you to the new season of Sail-ho Croatia. We’re setting off on yet another incredible journey sailing the Adriatic starting from the lush and picturesque Neretva valley and the town of Ploče. Stay tuned and enjoy the ride!

programme.title: Croatia Yacht Show & International Charter Expo - Highlight
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Dis.Traction
programme.desc: The two-time world wave sailing champion Marcilio Browne, originally from Brazil, has decided to make the Hawaiian island of Maui his permanent home to pursue his passion. Dis.Traction follows Marcilio during his first training sessions leading into to the decisive season’s final contest where the greatest windsurfers compete for the ultimate prize.Following the end of contest season, Marcilio and fellow wave sailing champion Morgan Noireaux take to the mountains, or rather to the volcano for some alternative action. As the island is exposed to heavy ground swells from many directions, Marcilio and his friends are always looking for ideal spots to practice.Dis.Traction is a rollercoaster ride of action and an authentic insight into the perspective of a seasoned professional windsurfer. Shot on location it features the breathtaking beauty of the Hawaiian Island of Maui and showcases appearances of some of the sport’s greatest athletes.

programme.title: Hempel World Cup Series - Netherlands
programme.desc: - Array

programme.title: Why Not Whitewater?
programme.desc: Welcome to Dimaro, the Rafting Center Val di Sole. Our story begins in 1982 when a group of adventurers decided to make history by being the first humans to raft down the River Noce. Not only they managed to go down the river against all odds, but they also fell in love with its beautiful landscapes and decided to share it with the rest of the world.Why Not Whitewater? takes us on a journey to the north of Italy to explore the valleys and rivers in the most adventurous ways. From canoening to kayaking and from rafting to hydrospeed, we are set to live an odyssey filled with adrenaline and fun that will leave you wondeing why not white water this summer!

programme.desc: Since the first wave was ridden, surfers have shared stories and experiences to try to explain the wonders inside the waves they ride.Professional and amateur surfers, photographers and videographers around the world have the possibility to display their passion: Barrel surfing. This term is used by surfers to describe riding well inside the curve of a finely shaped breaking wave, for many described as magical.The Barrel is all about surfer’s skills, creativity and love for adventure as they look for the best waves for tube riding. Get inside the barrel of the wave, with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Bass By Kayak
programme.desc: Bass by Kayak follows a small expedition party formed by four adventurers crossing from the Australian mainland to Tasmania in sea kayaks. With a 300 km separation, a bare four hundred people have made the crossing of the Bass Strait in the modern era. Before then, Aboriginal Australians sailed in their canoes the shocking distance during the last ice age.This adventure makes strategic stops on the islands along the way; however, it is one of the toughest and deadliest journeys in the world. Adventure filmmaker Beau Miles narrates the expedition as the first ever feature film of the crossing via sea kayaks.The travellers will experience chafe, storms, bad cooking and an emerging mate-ship between men that barely know each other.Watch it now with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Dis.Traction
programme.desc: The two-time world wave sailing champion Marcilio Browne, originally from Brazil, has decided to make the Hawaiian island of Maui his permanent home to pursue his passion. Dis.Traction follows Marcilio during his first training sessions leading into to the decisive season’s final contest where the greatest windsurfers compete for the ultimate prize.Following the end of contest season, Marcilio and fellow wave sailing champion Morgan Noireaux take to the mountains, or rather to the volcano for some alternative action. As the island is exposed to heavy ground swells from many directions, Marcilio and his friends are always looking for ideal spots to practice.Dis.Traction is a rollercoaster ride of action and an authentic insight into the perspective of a seasoned professional windsurfer. Shot on location it features the breathtaking beauty of the Hawaiian Island of Maui and showcases appearances of some of the sport’s greatest athletes.

programme.title: America's Cup Insight - Episode 1
programme.desc: We are getting ready for the AC35 and we want to look deeper on this event. We will dive into the Director's Cut before taking a quick look at the Challengers that will face off in the Louis Vuitton Cup. Then, we have a word with the defender team, Oracle Team USA before talking with challenger Franck Cammas and Groupama Team France.

programme.title: America's Cup Insight - Episode 2
programme.desc: Welcome to another episode of America's Cup Insight. Today, we are at the heart of the British challenge inside the Land Rover B.A.R. We will discover more about the team, the players and the big family behind the boat. Also, find out how boundaries are changing the game and find out more about the wings of change.Plunge into the action with AC35 Insight.

programme.title: Meet me in Madeira
programme.desc: Welcome to Madeira!The main island of the volcanic Portuguese archipelago is home to a wide sea life and rich ecosystem. With its subtropical climate, Madeira is a chosen destination for nautical sports enthusiasts that enjoy the paradisiac conditions of the island.From diving to surfing, sailing to coasteering, Madeira offers a wide variety of nautical activities for the avid sportsmen or just the visiting tourist. Join Andi van Zyl on an experience that only this Portuguese archipelago has to offer, from stand-up paddling to wine tasting.Discover the secrets of Madeira and get to know the heart-warming locals of the beautiful archipelago in an exclusive show on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Surf's Cooking
programme.desc: Matt Bromley is a renowned big wave surfer. Ollie Swart is a chef. Together, the two best friends will embark on an adventure down Cape Town’s coastline. Foraging for food and looking for the best surf spots, Surf’s Cooking brings gastronomy to the surfing world with this beautiful friendship.Both friends will have to quickly adapt and learn about each other. For Matt Bromley, learning about foraging for food and how to cook it properly will become challenging, and Ollie Swart will have to surf past the obstacles in this nautical sport. Will they be ready to share their passion between them?Watch their adventure in South Africa on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: SXM Best Weekend
programme.desc: Ready for the best weekend of your lives? The SXM Best Weekend is one of the most anticipated Caribbean summer events of the year. A local group of young brothers and musicians called “The Red Eye Crew” decided to revolutionize the party scenario in these paradisiac islands. That is why they brought a party wave onto St. Maarten.Follow their crazy adventure as they organize this explosive event and share their inspiring story on how they came up with this idea and what drives them. A fun journey for party lovers, SXM Best Weekend will have you put your hands up and enjoy the queens of Caribbean music with blasting performances from Haitian rapper Wyclef Jean.Party your way around the Caribbean rhythm, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Surfer's Blood
programme.desc: Renowned surf photographer and filmmaker Patrick Trefz explores the essence of the sport he loves by profiling those who inspire him. Surfers' Blood tells the universal story of true individuals that share deep bloodlines connected to the sea.From wooden fishing boats in the Basque Country coast with oar and surfboard shaper Patxi Oliden to the metropolis of San Francisco and the eccentric computer shapes of Apple fame designer Thomas Meyerhoffer, unique people unite by a deep connection to the sea.Watch it on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 7
programme.desc: The first Chris Craft boat was built by Christopher Colombus Smith in 1874, and since the brand has left its mark on many. Now we have the chance to try the Capri 27, built with modern materials but the same care and precision as the old master carpenters. Then, we will discover Eleva Yachts in the Marina degli Aregai before getting onboard the Benetti 125 Rolls Royce Azipull. Come join us!

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 8
programme.desc: In today's episode of The Boat Show we get on board a really special superyacht. Built by Benetti and propulsed by Rolls Royce we are on board the Benetti 125 Fast. Then, we get on board the Azimut S6 to remember the sport factor in a luxury yacht. Come join us on board!

programme.title: H2O Racing: Aquabike World Championship - Episode 1 Season Preview
programme.desc: Welcome to the 25th season of the UIM ABP Aquabike World Championship. The world's best jetski racers are once again looking for the UIM ABP World title in six categories: the Ski Division GP1, the Ski Ladies GP1, the Freestyle, the Runabout GP1 as well as the Ski GP Junior 3.3 and 3.2.They will prove their skills across different Grand Prixes around the world. The rider who gets the most points throughout the season will be crowned world champion.Get to know what to expect from this year's edition in this season preview.
